Oxlint.name: Oxlint
Oxlint.description: Oxlint is a high-performance linter for JavaScript and TypeScript built on the Oxc compiler stack.

accessor-pairs.description: Enforces getter/setter pairs in objects and classes.

array-callback-return.description: Enforce return statements in callbacks of array methods

arrow-body-style.description:
This rule can enforce or disallow the use of braces around arrow function body.
Arrow functions can use either:

block-scoped-var.description:
Enforces that variables are both declared and used within the same block scope.
This rule prevents accidental use of variables outside their intended block, mimicking C-style block scoping in JavaScript.

capitalized-comments.description: Enforces or disallows capitalization of the first letter of a comment.

class-methods-use-this.description: Enforce that class methods utilize this.

complexity.description:
Enforces a maximum cyclomatic complexity in a program, which is the number
of linearly independent paths in a program.

constructor-super.description:
Requires super() calls in constructors of derived classes and disallows super() calls
in constructors of non-derived classes.

curly.description:
This rule enforces the use of curly braces {} for all control statements
(if, else, for, while, do, with).
It ensures that all blocks are enclosed in curly braces to improve code clarity and maintainability.

default-case.description:
Enforces that all switch statements include a default case,
unless explicitly marked with a configured comment.

default-case-last.description: Requires the default clause in switch statements to be the last one.

default-param-last.description: Requires default parameters in functions to be the last ones.

eqeqeq.description: Requires the use of the === and !== operators, disallowing the use of == and !=.

for-direction.description:
Disallow for loops where the update clause moves the counter in the wrong
direction, preventing the loop from reaching its stop condition.

func-names.description: Require or disallow named function expressions.

func-style.description: Enforce the consistent use of either function declarations or expressions assigned to variables.

getter-return.description: Requires all getters to have a return statement.

grouped-accessor-pairs.description: Require grouped accessor pairs in object literals and classes

guard-for-in.description: Require for-in loops to include an if statement.

id-length.description:
This rule enforces a minimum and/or maximum identifier length convention by counting the
graphemes for a given identifier.

init-declarations.description: Require or disallow initialization in variable declarations.

max-classes-per-file.description: Enforce a maximum number of classes per file

max-depth.description:
Enforce a maximum depth that blocks can be nested. This rule helps to limit the complexity
of nested blocks, improving readability and maintainability by ensuring that code does not
become too deeply nested.

max-lines.description: Enforce a maximum number of lines per file.

max-lines-per-function.description:
Enforce a maximum number of lines of code in a function. This rule ensures
that functions do not exceed a specified line count, promoting smaller,
more focused functions that are easier to maintain and understand.

max-nested-callbacks.description:
Enforce a maximum depth that callbacks can be nested. This rule helps to limit
the complexity of callback nesting, ensuring that callbacks do not become too
deeply nested, improving code readability and maintainability.

max-params.description:
Enforce a maximum number of parameters in function definitions which by
default is three.

max-statements.description:
Enforce a maximum number of statements in a function. This rule ensures
that functions do not exceed a specified statements count, promoting smaller,
more focused functions that are easier to maintain and understand.

new-cap.description: This rule requires constructor names to begin with a capital letter.

no-alert.description: Disallow the use of alert, confirm, and prompt

no-array-constructor.description: Disallows creating arrays with the Array constructor.

no-async-promise-executor.description: Disallow using an async function as a Promise executor.

no-await-in-loop.description: This rule disallows the use of await within loop bodies. (for, for-in, for-of, while, do-while).

no-caller.description: Disallow the use of arguments.caller or arguments.callee.

no-case-declarations.description: Disallow lexical declarations in case clauses.

no-cond-assign.description: Disallow assignment operators in conditional expressions

no-constant-binary-expression.description: Disallow expressions where the operation doesn't affect the value

no-control-regex.description:
Disallows control characters and some escape sequences that match
control characters in regular expressions.

no-debugger.description: Checks for usage of the debugger statement

no-delete-var.description:
The purpose of the delete operator is to remove a property from an
object.

no-div-regex.description: Disallow equal signs explicitly at the beginning of regular expressions.

no-dupe-else-if.description: Disallow duplicate conditions in if-else-if chains

no-dupe-keys.description: Disallow duplicate keys in object literals.

no-else-return.description: Disallow else blocks after return statements in if statements

no-empty-character-class.description: Disallow empty character classes in regular expressions

no-empty-function.description: Disallows the usage of empty functions.

no-empty-static-block.description: Disallows the usages of empty static blocks

no-eq-null.description: Disallow null comparisons without type-checking operators.

no-eval.description:
Disallows referencing the eval function. This rule is aimed at preventing
potentially dangerous, unnecessary, and slow code by disallowing the use of
the eval() function.

no-ex-assign.description: Disallow reassigning exceptions in catch clauses

no-extend-native.description:
Prevents extending native global objects such as Object, String, or Array with new
properties.

no-extra-boolean-cast.description: This rule disallows unnecessary boolean casts.

no-global-assign.description: Disallow modifications to read-only global variables.

no-implicit-coercion.description: Disallows shorthand type conversions using operators like !!, +, ""+ , etc.

no-inline-comments.description: Disallows comments on the same line as code.

no-inner-declarations.description: Disallow variable or function declarations in nested blocks.

no-invalid-regexp.description: Disallow invalid regular expression strings in RegExp constructors.

no-irregular-whitespace.description: Disallows the use of irregular whitespace characters in the code.

no-iterator.description: Disallow the use of the __iterator__ property.

no-label-var.description: Disallow labels that share a name with a variable.

no-lonely-if.description: Disallow if statements as the only statement in if blocks without else.

no-loop-func.description:
Disallows function declarations and expressions inside loop statements
when they reference variables declared in the outer scope that may change
across iterations.

no-loss-of-precision.description: Disallow precision loss of number literal.

no-magic-numbers.description:
This rule aims to make code more readable and refactoring easier by ensuring that special numbers are declared as constants to make their meaning explicit.
The current implementation does not support BigInt numbers inside array indexes.

no-misleading-character-class.description: This rule reports regular expressions which include multiple code point characters in character class syntax. This includes:

no-multi-assign.description: Disallow use of chained assignment expressions.

no-nested-ternary.description:
This rule disallows deeply nested ternary expressions.
Nested ternary expressions that are only one level deep and wrapped in parentheses are allowed.

no-new.description: Disallow new operators outside of assignments or comparisons.

no-new-func.description: The rule disallow new operators with the Function object.

no-new-native-nonconstructor.description: Disallow new operators with global non-constructor functions (Symbol, BigInt).

no-new-wrappers.description: Disallow new operators with the String, Number, and Boolean objects

no-nonoctal-decimal-escape.description: This rule disallows \8 and \9 escape sequences in string literals

no-obj-calls.description: Disallow calling some global objects as functions.

no-object-constructor.description: Disallow calls to the Object constructor without an argument

no-param-reassign.description: Disallow reassigning function parameters or, optionally, their properties.

no-promise-executor-return.description: Disallow returning values from Promise executor functions.

no-proto.description: Disallow the use of the __proto__ property.

no-prototype-builtins.description: Disallow calling some Object.prototype methods directly on objects

no-redeclare.description:
This rule disallows redeclaring variables within the same scope, ensuring that each variable
is declared only once. It helps avoid confusion and unintended behavior in code.

no-regex-spaces.description: Disallow 2+ consecutive spaces in regular expressions.

no-restricted-globals.description: This rule allows you to specify global variable names that you don't want to use in your application.

no-restricted-imports.description:
This rule allows you to specify imports that you don’t want to use in your application.
It applies to static imports only, not dynamic ones.

no-return-assign.description: Disallows assignment operators in return statements.

no-self-assign.description: Disallow assignments where both sides are exactly the same.

no-self-compare.description: Disallow comparisons where both sides are exactly the same

no-sequences.description: Disallows the use of the comma operator.

no-shadow.description: Disallows variable declarations from shadowing variables declared in the outer scope.

no-shadow-restricted-names.description:
Disallows the redefining of global variables such as undefined, NaN, Infinity,
eval, globalThis and arguments.

no-template-curly-in-string.description:
Disallow template literal placeholder syntax in regular strings. This rule ensures that
expressions like ${variable} are only used within template literals, avoiding incorrect
usage in regular strings.

no-this-before-super.description: Requires calling super() before using this or super.

no-throw-literal.description: Disallows throwing literals or non-Error objects as exceptions.

no-unassigned-vars.description: Disallow let or var variables that are read but never assigned

no-undef.description: Disallow the use of undeclared variables.

no-undefined.description: Disallow the use of undefined as an identifier

no-unexpected-multiline.description:
In most cases, semicolons are not required in JavaScript in order for code to be parsed
and executed as expected. Typically this occurs because semicolons are automatically
inserted based on a fixed set of rules. This rule exists to detect those cases where a semicolon
is NOT inserted automatically, and may be parsed differently than expected.

no-unmodified-loop-condition.description: Disallow references in loop conditions that are never modified within the loop.

no-unneeded-ternary.description: Disallow ternary operators when simpler alternatives exist

no-unreachable.description: Disallow unreachable code after return, throw, continue, and break statements.

no-unsafe-finally.description: Disallow control flow statements in finally blocks.

no-unsafe-negation.description:
Disallows negating the left operand of relational operators to prevent logical errors
caused by misunderstanding operator precedence or accidental use of negation.

no-unsafe-optional-chaining.description: Disallow use of optional chaining in contexts where the undefined value is not allowed

no-unused-vars.description:
Disallows variable declarations, imports, or type declarations that are
not used in code.

no-use-before-define.description: Disallows using variables before they are defined.

no-useless-backreference.description:
Disallows backreferences in regular expressions that will always be ignored
because the capture group they refer to has not matched and cannot match
at the time the backreference is evaluated.

no-useless-computed-key.description: Disallow unnecessary computed property keys in objects and classes

no-useless-concat.description: Disallow unnecessary concatenation of literals or template literals

no-useless-constructor.description: Disallow constructors that can be safely removed without changing how the class works.

no-useless-rename.description: Disallow renaming import, export, and destructured assignments to the same name.

no-var.description:
ECMAScript 2015 allows programmers to create variables with block scope
instead of function scope using the let and const keywords. Block
scope is common in many other programming languages and helps
programmers avoid mistakes.

no-void.description: Disallows the use of the void operator.

no-warning-comments.description: Disallows warning comments such as TODO, FIXME, XXX in code.

operator-assignment.description:
This rule requires or disallows assignment operator shorthand where possible.
It encourages the use of shorthand assignment operators like +=, -=, *=, /=, etc.
to make the code more concise and readable.

prefer-const.description:
Requires const declarations for variables that are never
reassigned after their initial declaration.

prefer-destructuring.description: Require destructuring from arrays and/or objects

prefer-exponentiation-operator.description: Disallow the use of Math.pow in favor of the ** operator

prefer-numeric-literals.description:
Disallow parseInt() and Number.parseInt() in favor of binary, octal, and hexadecimal
literals.

prefer-object-has-own.description: Disallow use of Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call() and prefer use of Object.hasOwn()

prefer-object-spread.description: Disallow using Object.assign with an object literal as the first argument and prefer the use of object spread instead

prefer-promise-reject-errors.description: This rule enforces passing an Error object to Promise.reject().

prefer-rest-params.description: Disallows the use of the arguments object and instead enforces the use of rest parameters.

prefer-spread.description: Enforces the use of the spread operator (...) over outdated patterns.

prefer-template.description: Require template literals instead of string concatenation.

preserve-caught-error.description:
Enforces that when re-throwing an error in a catch block, the original error
is preserved using the 'cause' property.

radix.description:
Enforce the consistent use of the radix argument when using parseInt(),
which specifies what base to use for parsing the number.

require-await.description: This rule disallows async functions which do not have an await expression.

require-yield.description: This rule generates warnings for generator functions that do not have the yield keyword.

sort-imports.description:
This rule checks all import declarations and verifies that all imports are first sorted
by the used member syntax and then alphabetically by the first member or alias name.

sort-keys.description:
When declaring multiple properties, sorting property names alphabetically makes it easier
to find and/or diff necessary properties at a later time.

sort-vars.description:
When declaring multiple variables within the same block, sorting variable names make it
easier to find necessary variable easier at a later time.

unicode-bom.description: Require or disallow Unicode byte order mark (BOM)

use-isnan.description: Disallows checking against NaN without using isNaN() call.

valid-typeof.description: Enforce comparing typeof expressions against valid strings.

vars-on-top.description: Enforces that all var declarations are placed at the top of their containing scope.

yoda.description:
Require or disallow "Yoda" conditions.
This rule aims to enforce consistent style of conditions which compare a variable to a literal value.

consistent-type-specifier-style.description: This rule either enforces or bans the use of inline type-only markers for named imports.

default.description:
If a default import is requested, this rule will report if there is no
default export in the imported module.

export.description: Reports funny business with exports, like repeated exports of names or defaults.

exports-last.description:
This rule enforces that all exports are declared at the bottom of the file.
This rule will report any export declarations that comes before any non-export statements.

extensions.description:
Some file resolve algorithms allow you to omit the file extension within the import source path.
For example the node resolver (which does not yet support ESM/import) can resolve ./foo/bar to the absolute path /User/someone/foo/bar.js because the .js extension is resolved automatically by default in CJS.
Depending on the resolver you can configure more extensions to get resolved automatically.
In order to provide a consistent use of file extensions across your code base, this rule can enforce or disallow the use of certain file extensions.

first.description: Forbids any non-import statements before imports except directives.

group-exports.description:
Reports when named exports are not grouped together in a single export declaration
or when multiple assignments to CommonJS module.exports
or exports object are present in a single file.

max-dependencies.description: Forbid modules to have too many dependencies (import statements only).

named.description:
Verifies that all named imports are part of the set of named exports in
the referenced module.

namespace.description:
Enforces names exist at the time they are dereferenced, when imported as
a full namespace (i.e. import * as foo from './foo'; foo.bar(); will
report if bar is not exported by ./foo.). Will report at the import
declaration if there are no exported names found. Also, will report for
computed references (i.e. foo["bar"]()). Reports on assignment to a
member of an imported namespace.

no-absolute-path.description: This rule forbids the import of modules using absolute paths.

no-amd.description: Forbids the use of AMD require and define calls.

no-anonymous-default-export.description: Disallows anonymous functions and classes as default exports.

no-commonjs.description: Forbids the use of CommonJS require calls. Also forbids module.exports and exports.*.

no-cycle.description: Ensures that there is no resolvable path back to this module via its dependencies.

no-default-export.description:
Forbids a module from having default exports. This helps your editor
provide better auto-import functionality, as named exports offer more
explicit and predictable imports compared to default exports.

no-duplicates.description:
Reports if a resolved path is imported more than once in the same module.
This helps avoid unnecessary duplicate imports and keeps the code clean.

no-dynamic-require.description:
Forbids imports that use an expression for the module argument. This includes
dynamically resolved paths in require or import statements.

no-empty-named-blocks.description: Enforces that named import blocks are not empty.

no-mutable-exports.description: Forbids the use of mutable exports with var or let.

no-named-as-default.description:
Reports use of an exported name as the locally imported name of a default export.
This happens when an imported default export is assigned a name that conflicts
with a named export from the same module.

no-named-as-default-member.description:
Reports the use of an exported name (named export) as a property on the
default export. This occurs when trying to access a named export through
the default export, which is incorrect.

no-named-default.description: Reports use of a default export as a locally named import.

no-nodejs-modules.description: Forbid the use of Node.js builtin modules. Can be useful for client-side web projects that do not have access to those modules.

no-relative-parent-imports.description: Forbids importing modules from parent directories using relative paths.

no-self-import.description:
Forbids a module from importing itself. This can sometimes happen accidentally,
especially during refactoring.

no-unassigned-import.description: This rule aims to remove modules with side-effects by reporting when a module is imported but not assigned.

no-webpack-loader-syntax.description: Forbids using Webpack loader syntax directly in import or require statements.

prefer-default-export.description: In exporting files, this rule checks if there is default export or not.

unambiguous.description:
Warn if a module could be mistakenly parsed as a script instead of
as a pure ES module.

consistent-test-it.description:
Jest allows you to choose how you want to define your tests, using the it or
the test keywords, with multiple permutations for each:

expect-expect.description: This rule triggers when there is no call made to expect in a test, ensure that there is at least one expect call made in a test.

max-expects.description: This rule enforces a maximum number of expect() calls in a single test.

max-nested-describe.description: This rule enforces a maximum depth to nested describe() calls.

no-alias-methods.description: This rule ensures that only the canonical name as used in the Jest documentation is used in the code.

no-commented-out-tests.description:
This rule raises a warning about commented out tests. It's similar to the
no-disabled-tests rule.

no-conditional-expect.description:
This rule prevents the use of expect in conditional blocks, such as ifs & catch(s).
This includes using expect in callbacks to functions named catch, which are assumed to be promises.

no-deprecated-functions.description:
Over the years Jest has accrued some debt in the form of functions that have
either been renamed for clarity, or replaced with more powerful APIs.

no-disabled-tests.description: This rule raises a warning about disabled tests.

no-done-callback.description: This rule checks the function parameter of hooks & tests for use of the done argument, suggesting you return a promise instead.

no-duplicate-hooks.description: Disallows duplicate hooks in describe blocks.

no-export.description: Prevents using exports if a file has one or more tests in it.

no-focused-tests.description:
This rule reminds you to remove .only from your tests by raising a warning
whenever you are using the exclusivity feature.

no-hooks.description: Disallows Jest setup and teardown hooks, such as beforeAll.

no-identical-title.description:
This rule looks at the title of every test and test suite.
It will report when two test suites or two test cases at the same level of a test suite have the same title.

no-interpolation-in-snapshots.description: Prevents the use of string interpolations in snapshots.

no-jasmine-globals.description:
This rule reports on any usage of Jasmine globals, which is not ported to
Jest, and suggests alternatives from Jest's own API.

no-mocks-import.description: This rule reports imports from a path containing a mocks component.

no-restricted-jest-methods.description: Restrict the use of specific jest and vi methods.

no-restricted-matchers.description: Ban specific matchers & modifiers from being used, and can suggest alternatives.

no-standalone-expect.description:
Prevents expect statements outside of a test or it block. An expect
within a helper function (but outside of a test or it block) will not
trigger this rule.

no-test-prefixes.description: Require using .only and .skip over f and x.

no-unneeded-async-expect-function.description: Disallows unnecessary async function wrapper for expected promises.

no-untyped-mock-factory.description:
This rule triggers a warning if mock() or doMock() is used without a generic
type parameter or return type.

padding-around-test-blocks.description:
This rule enforces a line of padding before and after 1 or more
test/it statements.

prefer-comparison-matcher.description:
This rule checks for comparisons in tests that could be replaced with one of the
following built-in comparison matchers:

prefer-each.description: This rule enforces using each rather than manual loops.

prefer-equality-matcher.description:
Jest has built-in matchers for expecting equality, which allow for more readable
tests and error messages if an expectation fails.

prefer-expect-resolves.description:
Prefer await expect(...).resolves over expect(await ...) when testing
promises.

prefer-hooks-in-order.description: Ensures that hooks are in the order that they are called in.

prefer-hooks-on-top.description:
While hooks can be setup anywhere in a test file, they are always called in a
specific order, which means it can be confusing if they're intermixed with test
cases.

prefer-jest-mocked.description:
When working with mocks of functions using Jest, it's recommended to use the
jest.mocked() helper function to properly type the mocked functions. This rule
enforces the use of jest.mocked() for better type safety and readability.

prefer-lowercase-title.description:
Enforce it, test, describe, and bench to have descriptions that begin with a
lowercase letter. This provides more readable test failures.

prefer-mock-promise-shorthand.description:
When working with mocks of functions that return promises, Jest provides some
API sugar functions to reduce the amount of boilerplate you have to write.
These methods should be preferred when possible.

prefer-mock-return-shorthand.description: When working with mocks of functions that return simple values, Jest provides some API sugar functions to reduce the amount of boilerplate you have to write.

prefer-spy-on.description:
When mocking a function by overwriting a property you have to manually restore
the original implementation when cleaning up. When using jest.spyOn() Jest
keeps track of changes, and they can be restored with jest.restoreAllMocks(),
mockFn.mockRestore() or by setting restoreMocks to true in the Jest
config.

prefer-strict-equal.description: This rule triggers a warning if toEqual() is used to assert equality.

prefer-to-be.description:
Recommends using toBe matcher for primitive literals and specific
matchers for null, undefined, and NaN.

prefer-to-contain.description:
In order to have a better failure message, toContain() should be used upon
asserting expectations on an array containing an object.

prefer-to-have-been-called.description: Suggests using toHaveBeenCalled() or not.toHaveBeenCalled() over toHaveBeenCalledTimes(0) or toBeCalledTimes(0).

prefer-to-have-been-called-times.description:
In order to have a better failure message, toHaveBeenCalledTimes should be used
instead of directly checking the length of mock.calls.

prefer-to-have-length.description:
In order to have a better failure message, toHaveLength() should be used upon
asserting expectations on objects length property.

prefer-todo.description:
When test cases are empty then it is better to mark them as test.todo as it
will be highlighted in the summary output.

require-hook.description:
This rule flags any expression that is either at the toplevel of a test file or
directly within the body of a describe, except for the following:

require-to-throw-message.description: This rule triggers a warning if toThrow() or toThrowError() is used without an error message.

require-top-level-describe.description: Requires test cases and hooks to be inside a top-level describe block.

valid-describe-callback.description:
This rule validates that the second parameter of a describe() function is a
callback function. This callback function:

valid-expect.description: Checks that expect() is called correctly.

valid-title.description: Checks that the titles of Jest and Vitest blocks are valid.

check-access.description: Checks that @access tags use one of the following values:

check-property-names.description: Ensures that property names in JSDoc are not duplicated on the same block and that nested properties have defined roots.

check-tag-names.description:
Reports invalid block tag names.
Additionally checks for tag names that are redundant when using a type checker such as TypeScript.

empty-tags.description: Expects the following tags to be empty of any content:

implements-on-classes.description: Reports an issue with any non-constructor function using @implements.

no-defaults.description:
This rule reports defaults being used on the relevant portion of @param or @default.
It also optionally reports the presence of the square-bracketed optional arguments at all.

require-param.description: Requires that all function parameters are documented with JSDoc @param tags.

require-param-description.description: Requires that each @param tag has a description value.

require-param-name.description: Requires that all @param tags have names.

require-param-type.description: Requires that each @param tag has a type value (within curly brackets).

require-property.description:
Requires that all @typedef and @namespace tags have @property tags
when their type is a plain object, Object, or PlainObject.

require-property-description.description: Requires that all @property tags have descriptions.

require-property-name.description: Requires that all @property tags have names.

require-property-type.description: Requires that each @property tag has a type value (within curly brackets).

require-returns.description:
Requires that return statements are documented.
Will also report if multiple @returns tags are present.

require-returns-description.description:
Requires that the @returns tag has a description value.
The error will not be reported if the return value is void or undefined or if it is Promise<void> or Promise<undefined>.

require-returns-type.description: Requires that @returns tag has a type value (in curly brackets).

require-yields.description:
Requires that yields are documented.
Will also report if multiple @yields tags are present.

alt-text.description:
Enforce that all elements that require alternative text have meaningful
information to relay back to the end user.

anchor-ambiguous-text.description: Inspects anchor link text for the use of ambiguous words.

anchor-has-content.description:
Enforce that anchors have content and that the content is accessible to screen readers.
Accessible means that it is not hidden using the aria-hidden prop.

anchor-is-valid.description:
The HTML <a> element, with a valid href attribute, is formally defined as representing a hyperlink.
That is, a link between one HTML document and another, or between one location inside an HTML document and another location inside the same document.

aria-activedescendant-has-tabindex.description: Enforce elements with aria-activedescendant are tabbable.

aria-props.description: Enforces that elements do not use invalid ARIA attributes.

aria-proptypes.description: Enforces that elements do not use invalid ARIA state and property values.

aria-role.description:
Elements with ARIA roles must use a valid, non-abstract ARIA role. A
reference to role definitions can be found at
WAI-ARIA site.

aria-unsupported-elements.description:
Enforces that reserved DOM elements do not contain ARIA roles, states,
or properties.

autocomplete-valid.description: Enforces that an element's autocomplete attribute must be a valid value.

click-events-have-key-events.description: Enforce onClick is accompanied by at least one of the following: onKeyUp, onKeyDown, onKeyPress.

heading-has-content.description:
Enforce that heading elements (h1, h2, etc.) have content and
that the content is accessible to screen readers.
Accessible means that it is not hidden using the aria-hidden prop.

html-has-lang.description: Ensures that every HTML document has a lang attribute

iframe-has-title.description: Enforce iframe elements have a title attribute.

img-redundant-alt.description:
Enforce that img alt attributes do not contain redundant words like
"image", "picture", or "photo".

label-has-associated-control.description: Enforce that a label tag has a text label and an associated control.

lang.description: The lang prop on the <html> element must be a valid IETF's BCP 47 language tag.

media-has-caption.description:
Checks if <audio> and <video> elements have a <track> element for captions.
This ensures media content is accessible to all users, including those with hearing impairments.

mouse-events-have-key-events.description: Enforce onMouseOver/onMouseOut are accompanied by onFocus/onBlur.

no-access-key.description: Enforces that the accessKey prop is not used on any element to avoid complications with keyboard commands used by a screenreader.

no-aria-hidden-on-focusable.description: Enforces that aria-hidden="true" is not set on focusable elements.

no-autofocus.description: Enforce that autoFocus prop is not used on elements.

no-distracting-elements.description: Enforces that no distracting elements are used.

no-noninteractive-tabindex.description: This rule checks that non-interactive elements don't have a tabIndex which would make them interactive via keyboard navigation.

no-redundant-roles.description:
Enforces that code does not include a redundant role property, in the
case that it's identical to the implicit role property of the
element type.

no-static-element-interactions.description: Enforces that static HTML elements with event handlers must have appropriate ARIA roles.

prefer-tag-over-role.description: Enforces using semantic HTML tags over role attribute.

role-has-required-aria-props.description:
Enforces that elements with ARIA roles must have all required attributes
for that role.

role-supports-aria-props.description:
Enforce that elements with explicit or implicit roles defined contain only aria-* properties supported by that role.
Many ARIA attributes (states and properties) can only be used on elements with particular roles.
Some elements have implicit roles, such as <a href="#" />, which will resolve to role="link".

scope.description: The scope prop should be used only on <th> elements.

tabindex-no-positive.description:
Enforces that positive values for the tabIndex attribute are not used
in JSX.

google-font-display.description: Enforce font-display behavior with Google Fonts.

google-font-preconnect.description: Enforces the presence of rel="preconnect" when using Google Fonts via <link> tags.

inline-script-id.description: Enforces that all next/script components with inline content or dangerouslySetInnerHTML must have an id prop.

next-script-for-ga.description:
Enforces the use of the next/script component when implementing Google Analytics in Next.js applications,
instead of using regular <script> tags.

no-assign-module-variable.description: Prevents the assignment or declaration of variables named module in Next.js applications.

no-async-client-component.description:
Prevents the use of async functions for client components in Next.js applications.
This rule checks for any async function that:

no-before-interactive-script-outside-doc.description:
Prevents the usage of next/script's beforeInteractive strategy outside of pages/_document.js.
This rule ensures that scripts with the beforeInteractive loading strategy are only used in the
document component where they are most effective.

no-css-tags.description:
Prevents manual inclusion of stylesheets using <link> tags in Next.js applications.
This rule checks for <link> tags with rel="stylesheet" that reference local CSS files.

no-document-import-in-page.description: Prevent importing next/document outside of pages/_document.js.

no-duplicate-head.description: Prevent duplicate usage of <Head> in pages/_document.js.

no-head-element.description: Prevents the usage of the native <head> element inside a Next.js application.

no-head-import-in-document.description: Prevents the usage of next/head inside a Next.js document.

no-html-link-for-pages.description: Prevents the usage of <a> elements to navigate between Next.js pages.

no-img-element.description: Prevent the usage of element due to slower LCP and higher bandwidth.

no-script-component-in-head.description: Prevent usage of next/script in next/head component.

no-styled-jsx-in-document.description: Prevent usage of styled-jsx in pages/_document.js.

no-sync-scripts.description:
This rule prevents the use of synchronous <script> tags in Next.js applications.
It requires that any <script> tag with a src attribute must also have either
the async or defer attribute.

no-title-in-document-head.description: Prevent usage of <title> with Head component from next/document.

no-typos.description: Detects common typos in Next.js data fetching function names.

no-unwanted-polyfillio.description: Prevent use of unsafe polyfill.io domains and duplicate polyfills.

global-require.description: Require require() calls to be placed at top-level module scope

no-new-require.description: Warn about calling new on require.

approx-constant.description:
Disallows the use of approximate constants, instead preferring the use
of the constants in the Math object.

bad-array-method-on-arguments.description: This rule applies when an array method is called on the arguments object itself.

bad-bitwise-operator.description: This rule applies when bitwise operators are used where logical operators are expected.

bad-char-at-comparison.description: This rule warns when the return value of the charAt method is used to compare a string of length greater than 1.

bad-comparison-sequence.description: This rule applies when the comparison operator is applied two or more times in a row.

bad-min-max-func.description:
Checks whether the clamp function Math.min(Math.max(x, y), z) always evaluate to a
constant result because the arguments are in the wrong order.

bad-object-literal-comparison.description: Checks for comparisons between object and array literals.

bad-replace-all-arg.description: This rule warns when the replaceAll method is called with a regular expression that does not have the global flag (g).

branches-sharing-code.description: Checks if the if and else blocks contain shared code that can be moved out of the blocks.

const-comparisons.description: Checks for redundant or logically impossible comparisons. This includes:

double-comparisons.description: This rule checks for double comparisons in logical expressions.

erasing-op.description: Checks for erasing operations, e.g., x * 0.

missing-throw.description: Checks whether the throw keyword is missing in front of a new expression.

no-accumulating-spread.description: Prevents using object or array spreads on accumulators in Array.prototype.reduce() and in loops.

no-async-endpoint-handlers.description: Disallows the use of async functions as Express endpoint handlers.

no-barrel-file.description:
Disallow the use of barrel files where the file contains export * statements,
and the total number of modules exceed a threshold.

no-map-spread.description:
Disallow the use of object or array spreads in
Array.prototype.map
and
Array.prototype.flatMap
to add properties/elements to array items.

no-this-in-exported-function.description: Disallows the use of this in exported functions.

number-arg-out-of-range.description: Checks whether the radix or precision arguments of number-related functions exceeds the limit.

only-used-in-recursion.description: Checks for arguments that are only used in recursion with no side-effects.

uninvoked-array-callback.description: This rule applies when an Array function has a callback argument used for an array with empty slots.

always-return.description:
Require returning inside each then() to create readable and reusable Promise chains.
We also allow someone to throw inside a then() which is essentially the same as return Promise.reject().

avoid-new.description: Disallow creating promises with new Promise().

catch-or-return.description:
Ensure that each time a then() is applied to a promise, a catch()
must be applied as well. Exceptions are made for promises returned from
a function.

no-callback-in-promise.description:
Disallows calling a callback function (cb()) inside a Promise.prototype.then()
or Promise.prototype.catch().

no-multiple-resolved.description: This rule warns of paths that resolve multiple times in executor functions that Promise constructors.

no-nesting.description: Disallow nested then() or catch() statements.

no-new-statics.description: Disallows calling new on static Promise methods.

no-promise-in-callback.description: Disallows the use of Promises within error-first callback functions.

no-return-in-finally.description: Disallow return statements in a finally() callback of a promise.

no-return-wrap.description:
Prevents unnecessary wrapping of return values in promises with either Promise.resolve
or Promise.reject.

param-names.description: Enforce standard parameter names for Promise constructors.

prefer-await-to-callbacks.description:
The rule encourages the use of async/await for handling asynchronous code
instead of traditional callback functions. async/await, introduced in ES2017,
provides a clearer and more concise syntax for writing asynchronous code,
making it easier to read and maintain.

prefer-await-to-then.description: Prefer await to then()/catch()/finally() for reading Promise values

prefer-catch.description:
Prefer catch to then(a, b) and then(null, b). This rule disallows the passing of an
argument into the second parameter of then calls for handling promise errors.

spec-only.description: Disallow use of non-standard Promise static methods.

valid-params.description: Enforces the proper number of arguments are passed to Promise functions.

button-has-type.description: Enforces explicit type attribute for all the button HTML elements.

checked-requires-onchange-or-readonly.description:
This rule enforces onChange or readOnly attribute for checked property of input elements.
It also warns when checked and defaultChecked properties are used together.

display-name.description: Enforces that React components have a displayName property.

exhaustive-deps.description: Verifies the list of dependencies for Hooks like useEffect and similar.

forbid-dom-props.description: This rule prevents passing of props to elements. This rule only applies to DOM Nodes (e.g.

forbid-elements.description: Allows you to configure a list of forbidden elements and to specify their desired replacements.

forward-ref-uses-ref.description:
Requires that components wrapped with forwardRef must have a ref parameter.
Omitting the ref argument is usually a bug,
and components not using ref don't need to be wrapped by forwardRef.

iframe-missing-sandbox.description: Enforce sandbox attribute on iframe elements

jsx-boolean-value.description: Enforce a consistent boolean attribute style in your code.

jsx-curly-brace-presence.description:
Disallow unnecessary JSX expressions when literals alone are
sufficient or enforce JSX expressions on literals in JSX children or
attributes.

jsx-filename-extension.description: Enforces consistent use of the .jsx file extension.

jsx-fragments.description: Enforces the shorthand or standard form for React Fragments.

jsx-handler-names.description: Ensures that any component or prop methods used to handle events are correctly prefixed.

jsx-key.description: Enforce key prop for elements in array

jsx-max-depth.description: Enforces a maximum depth for nested JSX elements and fragments.

jsx-no-comment-textnodes.description:
This rule prevents comment strings (e.g. beginning with // or /*) from being
accidentally injected as a text node in JSX statements.

jsx-no-constructed-context-values.description: Disallows JSX context provider values from taking values that will cause needless rerenders.

jsx-no-duplicate-props.description: This rule prevents duplicate props in JSX elements.

jsx-no-target-blank.description:
This rule aims to prevent user generated link hrefs and form actions from creating security vulnerabilities by
requiring rel='noreferrer' for external link hrefs and form actions, and optionally any dynamically generated
link hrefs and form actions.

jsx-pascal-case.description: Enforce PascalCase for user-defined JSX components

jsx-props-no-spread-multi.description: Enforces that any unique expression is only spread once.

no-array-index-key.description: Warn if an element uses an Array index in its key.

no-children-prop.description: Checks that children are not passed using a prop.

no-danger.description: This rule prevents the use of dangerouslySetInnerHTML prop.

no-danger-with-children.description: Disallows when a DOM element is using both children and dangerouslySetInnerHTML properties.

no-did-mount-set-state.description: Disallows using setState in the componentDidMount lifecycle method.

no-direct-mutation-state.description: This rule forbids the direct mutation of this.state in React components.

no-find-dom-node.description: This rule disallows the use of findDOMNode, which was deprecated in 2018 and removed in React 19.

no-is-mounted.description: This rule prevents using isMounted in class components.

no-multi-comp.description: Prevents multiple React components from being defined in the same file.

no-redundant-should-component-update.description: Disallow usage of shouldComponentUpdate when extending React.PureComponent.

no-render-return-value.description: This rule will warn you if you try to use the ReactDOM.render() return value.

no-set-state.description: Disallow the usage of this.setState in React components.

no-string-refs.description: This rule prevents using the deprecated behavior of string literals in ref attributes.

no-this-in-sfc.description: Prevents using this in stateless functional components.

no-unescaped-entities.description: This rule prevents characters that you may have meant as JSX escape characters from being accidentally injected as a text node in JSX statements.

no-unknown-property.description: Disallow usage of unknown DOM properties.

no-unsafe.description: This rule identifies and restricts the use of unsafe React lifecycle methods.

no-will-update-set-state.description: Disallows using setState in the componentWillUpdate lifecycle method.

only-export-components.description:
Ensures that modules only export React components (and related HMR-safe items) so
that Fast Refresh (a.k.a. hot reloading) can safely preserve component state.
Concretely, it validates the shape of your module’s exports and common entrypoints
(e.g. createRoot(...).render(<App />)) to match what integrations like
react-refresh expect.

prefer-es6-class.description:
React offers you two ways to create traditional components: using the
create-react-class package or the newer ES2015 class system.

react-in-jsx-scope.description: Enforces that React is imported and in-scope when using JSX syntax.

require-render-return.description: Enforce ES5 or ES2015 class for returning value in the render function.

rules-of-hooks.description:
Enforces the Rules of Hooks, ensuring that React Hooks are only called
in valid contexts and in the correct order.

self-closing-comp.description:
Detects components without children which can be self-closed to avoid
unnecessary extra closing tags.

state-in-constructor.description:
Enforces the state initialization style to be either in a
constructor or with a class property.

style-prop-object.description: Require that the value of the prop style be an object or a variable that is an object.

void-dom-elements-no-children.description:
Disallow void DOM elements (e.g. ,
) from receiving children.

jsx-no-jsx-as-prop.description:
Prevent JSX elements that are local to the current method from being
used as values of JSX props.

jsx-no-new-array-as-prop.description:
Prevent Arrays that are local to the current method from being used
as values of JSX props.

jsx-no-new-function-as-prop.description:
Prevent Functions that are local to the current method from being used
as values of JSX props.

jsx-no-new-object-as-prop.description:
Prevent Objects that are local to the current method from being used
as values of JSX props.

adjacent-overload-signatures.description: Require that function overload signatures be consecutive.

array-type.description: Require consistently using either T[] or Array<T> for arrays.

await-thenable.description: This rule disallows awaiting a value that is not a Thenable.

ban-ts-comment.description: This rule lets you set which directive comments you want to allow in your codebase.

ban-types.description: This rule bans specific types and can suggest alternatives. Note that it does not ban the corresponding runtime objects from being used.

class-literal-property-style.description: Enforces a consistent style for exposing literal values on classes.

consistent-generic-constructors.description: When constructing a generic class, you can specify the type arguments on either the left-hand side (as a type annotation) or the right-hand side (as part of the constructor call).

consistent-indexed-object-style.description: Choose between requiring either Record type or indexed signature types.

consistent-return.description: Enforce consistent return behavior in functions.

consistent-type-assertions.description: Enforce consistent usage of TypeScript type assertions.

consistent-type-definitions.description: Enforce type definitions to consistently use either interface or type.

consistent-type-exports.description: Enforce using export type for exports that are only used as types.

consistent-type-imports.description: Enforce consistent usage of type imports.

dot-notation.description: Enforce dot notation whenever property access can be written safely as obj.prop.

explicit-function-return-type.description: This rule enforces that functions have an explicit return type annotation.

explicit-module-boundary-types.description: Require explicit return and argument types on exported functions' and classes' public class methods.

no-array-delete.description: This rule disallows using the delete operator on array values.

no-base-to-string.description: This rule requires toString() and toLocaleString() calls to only be called on objects which provide useful information when stringified.

no-confusing-non-null-assertion.description: Disallow non-null assertion in locations that may be confusing.

no-confusing-void-expression.description: This rule forbids using void expressions in confusing locations such as arrow function returns.

no-duplicate-type-constituents.description: This rule disallows duplicate constituents of union or intersection types.

no-dynamic-delete.description: Disallow using the delete operator on computed key expressions.

no-empty-interface.description: Disallow the declaration of empty interfaces.

no-empty-object-type.description: To avoid confusion around the {} type allowing any non-nullish value, this rule bans usage of the {} type. That includes interfaces and object type aliases with no fields.

no-explicit-any.description: Disallows explicit use of the any type.

no-extraneous-class.description:
This rule reports when a class has no non-static members, such as for a
class used exclusively as a static namespace. This rule also reports
classes that have only a constructor and no fields. Those classes can
generally be replaced with a standalone function.

no-floating-promises.description: This rule disallows "floating" Promises in TypeScript code, which is a Promise that is created without any code to handle its resolution or rejection.

no-for-in-array.description: This rule disallows iterating over an array with a for-in loop.

no-implied-eval.description: This rule disallows the use of eval-like methods.

no-import-type-side-effects.description:
Enforce the use of top-level import type qualifier when an import only
has specifiers with inline type qualifiers.

no-inferrable-types.description: Disallow explicit type declarations for variables or parameters initialized to a number, string, or boolean

no-invalid-void-type.description: Disallow void type usage outside return types and configured generic contexts.

no-meaningless-void-operator.description: This rule disallows the void operator when its argument is already of type void or undefined.

no-misused-new.description:
Enforces valid definition of new and constructor. This rule prevents classes from defining
a method named new and interfaces from defining a method named constructor.

no-misused-promises.description:
This rule forbids providing Promises to logical locations such as if statements in places where the TypeScript
compiler allows them but they are not handled properly. These situations can often arise due to a missing
await keyword or just a misunderstanding of the way async functions are handled/awaited.

no-misused-spread.description: This rule disallows spreading syntax in places where it doesn't make sense or could cause runtime errors.

no-mixed-enums.description: This rule disallows enums from having both string and numeric members.

no-non-null-asserted-nullish-coalescing.description: Disallow non-null assertions in the left operand of a nullish coalescing operator.

no-non-null-asserted-optional-chain.description: Disallow non-null assertions after an optional chain expression.

no-non-null-assertion.description: Disallow non-null assertions using the ! postfix operator.

no-redundant-type-constituents.description: This rule disallows type constituents of unions and intersections that are redundant.

no-require-imports.description: Forbids the use of CommonJS require calls.

no-restricted-types.description: Disallow certain types from being used.

no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare.description: This rule disallows unnecessary equality comparisons with boolean literals.

no-unnecessary-condition.description:
Disallow conditions that are always truthy, always falsy, or always nullish
based on TypeScript's type information.

no-unnecessary-parameter-property-assign.description: Prevents unnecessary assignment of parameter properties.

no-unnecessary-qualifier.description: Disallow namespace qualifiers when the referenced name is already in scope.

no-unnecessary-template-expression.description: Disallows unnecessary template expressions (interpolations) that can be simplified.

no-unnecessary-type-arguments.description: This rule disallows type arguments that are identical to the default type parameter.

no-unnecessary-type-assertion.description: This rule disallows type assertions that do not change the type of an expression.

no-unnecessary-type-constraint.description: Disallow unnecessary constraints on generic types.

no-unnecessary-type-parameters.description: Disallow type parameters that are declared but not meaningfully used.

no-unsafe-argument.description: This rule disallows calling a function with an argument which is typed as any.

no-unsafe-assignment.description: This rule disallows assigning a value with type any to variables and properties.

no-unsafe-call.description: This rule disallows calling a value with type any.

no-unsafe-enum-comparison.description: This rule disallows comparing an enum value with a non-enum value.

no-unsafe-function-type.description: Disallow using the unsafe built-in Function type.

no-unsafe-member-access.description: This rule disallows member access on a value with type any.

no-unsafe-return.description: This rule disallows returning a value with type any from a function.

no-unsafe-type-assertion.description: Disallows unsafe type assertions that narrow a type.

no-unsafe-unary-minus.description: This rule disallows using the unary minus operator on a value which is not of type 'number' | 'bigint'.

no-useless-default-assignment.description: Disallow default assignments that can never be used.

no-useless-empty-export.description: Disallow empty exports that don't change anything in a module file.

no-var-requires.description: Disallow require statements except in import statements.

no-wrapper-object-types.description: Disallow the use of wrapper object types.

non-nullable-type-assertion-style.description: This rule prefers a non-null assertion over an explicit type cast for non-nullable types.

only-throw-error.description: This rule disallows throwing non-Error values.

parameter-properties.description: Requires or disallows parameter properties in class constructors.

prefer-as-const.description: Enforce the use of as const over literal type.

prefer-enum-initializers.description: Require each enum member value to be explicitly initialized.

prefer-find.description: Prefer .find(...) over .filter(...)[0] for retrieving a single element.

prefer-for-of.description: Enforces the use of for-of loop instead of a for loop with a simple iteration.

prefer-function-type.description: Enforce using function types instead of interfaces with call signatures.

prefer-includes.description:
Prefer includes() over indexOf() when checking for existence or non-existence.
All built-ins have .includes() in addition to .indexOf().

prefer-literal-enum-member.description: Explicit enum value must only be a literal value (string, number, boolean, etc).

prefer-namespace-keyword.description:
This rule reports when the module keyword is used instead of namespace.
This rule does not report on the use of TypeScript module declarations to describe external APIs (declare module 'foo' {}).

prefer-nullish-coalescing.description:
Enforce using the nullish coalescing operator (??) instead of logical OR (||)
or conditional expressions when the left operand might be null or undefined.

prefer-optional-chain.description:
Enforce using concise optional chain expressions instead of chained logical AND
operators, negated logical OR operators, or empty objects.

prefer-readonly.description: Require class members that are never reassigned to be marked readonly.

prefer-readonly-parameter-types.description: Require function and method parameters to use readonly-compatible types.

prefer-reduce-type-parameter.description: This rule prefers using a type parameter for the accumulator in Array.reduce instead of casting.

prefer-regexp-exec.description: Prefer RegExp#exec() over String#match() when extracting a regex match.

prefer-return-this-type.description: This rule enforces using this types for return types when possible.

prefer-string-starts-ends-with.description: Prefer String#startsWith() and String#endsWith() over using a regex with /^foo/ or /foo$/.

promise-function-async.description: This rule requires any function or method that returns a Promise to be marked as async.

related-getter-setter-pairs.description: This rule enforces that getters and setters for the same property are defined together and have related types.

require-array-sort-compare.description: This rule requires Array.sort() to be called with a comparison function.

restrict-plus-operands.description: This rule requires both operands of addition to be the same type and be number, string, or any.

restrict-template-expressions.description: This rule restricts the types allowed in template literal expressions.

return-await.description: This rule enforces consistent returning of awaited values from async functions.

strict-boolean-expressions.description: Disallow certain types in boolean expressions.

strict-void-return.description: Disallow returning non-void values where a void return is expected.

switch-exhaustiveness-check.description: This rule requires switch statements to be exhaustive when switching on union types.

triple-slash-reference.description: Disallow certain triple slash directives in favor of ES module import declarations.

unbound-method.description: This rule enforces unbound methods are called with their expected scope.

unified-signatures.description: Disallow overload signatures that can be unified into one.

use-unknown-in-catch-callback-variable.description: This rule enforces using unknown for catch clause variables instead of any.

catch-error-name.description:
This rule enforces consistent and descriptive naming for error variables
in catch statements, preventing the use of vague names like badName
or _ when the error is used.

consistent-assert.description: Enforces consistent usage of the assert module.

consistent-date-clone.description:
The Date constructor can clone a Date object directly when passed as an argument,
making timestamp conversion unnecessary. This rule enforces the use of the
direct Date cloning instead of using .getTime() for conversion.

consistent-empty-array-spread.description:
When spreading a ternary in an array, we can use both [] and '' as fallbacks,
but it's better to have consistent types in both branches.

consistent-existence-index-check.description:
Enforce consistent style for element existence checks with indexOf(),
lastIndexOf(), findIndex(), and findLastIndex(). This ensures
that comparisons are performed in a standard and clear way.

consistent-function-scoping.description:
Disallow functions that are declared in a scope which does not capture
any variables from the outer scope.

empty-brace-spaces.description:
Removes the extra spaces or new line characters inside a pair of braces
that does not contain additional code. This ensures that braces are clean
and do not contain unnecessary spaces or newlines.

error-message.description:
Enforces providing a message when creating built-in Error objects to
improve readability and debugging.

escape-case.description:
Enforces defining escape sequence values with uppercase characters rather than lowercase ones.
This promotes readability by making the escaped value more distinguishable from the identifier.

explicit-length-check.description: Enforce explicitly comparing the length or size property of a value.

filename-case.description:
Enforces a consistent case style for filenames to improve project organization and maintainability.
By default, kebab-case is enforced, but other styles can be configured.

new-for-builtins.description:
Enforces the use of new for the following builtins: Object, Array, ArrayBuffer, BigInt64Array,
BigUint64Array, DataView, Date, Error, Float32Array, Float64Array, Function, Int8Array,
Int16Array, Int32Array, Map, WeakMap, Set, WeakSet, Promise, RegExp, Uint8Array,
Uint16Array, Uint32Array, Uint8ClampedArray, SharedArrayBuffer, Proxy, WeakRef, FinalizationRegistry.

no-abusive-eslint-disable.description: Disallows oxlint-disable or eslint-disable comments without specifying rules.

no-accessor-recursion.description: Disallow recursive access to this within getters and setters

no-array-callback-reference.description: Prevents passing a function reference directly to iterator methods

no-array-for-each.description: Forbids the use of Array#forEach in favor of a for loop.

no-array-method-this-argument.description:
Disallows the use of the thisArg parameter in array iteration methods such as
map, filter, some, every, and similar.

no-await-expression-member.description: Disallows member access from await expressions.

no-await-in-promise-methods.description: Disallow using await in Promise method parameters

no-console-spaces.description: Disallows leading/trailing space inside console.log() and similar methods.

no-empty-file.description: Disallows files that do not contain any meaningful code.

no-hex-escape.description: Enforces a convention of using Unicode escapes instead of hexadecimal escapes for consistency and clarity.

no-immediate-mutation.description: Disallows mutating a variable immediately after initialization.

no-instanceof-array.description: Require Array.isArray() instead of instanceof Array.

no-instanceof-builtins.description: Disallows the use of instanceof with ECMAScript built-in constructors because:

no-invalid-fetch-options.description:
Disallow invalid options in fetch() and new Request(). Specifically, this rule ensures that
a body is not provided when the method is GET or HEAD, as it will result in a TypeError.

no-invalid-remove-event-listener.description: It warns when you use a non-function value as the second argument of removeEventListener.

no-length-as-slice-end.description: Disallow using length as the end argument of a slice call.

no-magic-array-flat-depth.description: Disallow magic numbers for Array.prototype.flat depth.

no-negation-in-equality-check.description: Disallow negated expressions on the left of (in)equality checks.

no-new-buffer.description: Disallows the deprecated new Buffer() constructor.

no-null.description: Disallow the use of the null literal, to encourage using undefined instead.

no-object-as-default-parameter.description: Disallow the use of an object literal as a default value for a parameter.

no-single-promise-in-promise-methods.description: Disallow passing single-element arrays to Promise methods

no-static-only-class.description: Disallow classes that only have static members.

no-this-assignment.description: Disallow assigning this to a variable.

no-unnecessary-array-splice-count.description: Disallows passing .length or Infinity as the deleteCount or skipCount argument of Array#splice() or Array#toSpliced().

no-unnecessary-slice-end.description:
Omitting the end argument defaults it to the object's .length.
Passing it explicitly or using Infinity is unnecessary

no-unreadable-iife.description: This rule disallows IIFEs with a parenthesized arrow function body.

no-useless-collection-argument.description: Disallow useless values or fallbacks in Set, Map, WeakSet, or WeakMap

no-useless-error-capture-stack-trace.description: Disallows unnecessary Error.captureStackTrace(…) in error constructors.

no-useless-fallback-in-spread.description: Disallow useless fallback when spreading in object literals.

no-useless-length-check.description: It checks for an unnecessary array length check in a logical expression.

no-useless-promise-resolve-reject.description: Disallows returning values wrapped in Promise.resolve or Promise.reject in an async function or a Promise#then/catch/finally callback.

no-useless-spread.description: Disallows using spread syntax in following, unnecessary cases:

no-useless-switch-case.description: Disallows useless default cases in switch statements.

no-zero-fractions.description: Prevents the use of zero fractions.

number-literal-case.description: This rule enforces proper case for numeric literals.

numeric-separators-style.description: Enforces a convention of grouping digits using numeric separators.

prefer-add-event-listener.description: Enforces the use of .addEventListener() and .removeEventListener() over their on-function counterparts.

prefer-array-find.description:
Encourages using Array.prototype.find instead of filter(...)[0] or
similar patterns when only the first matching element is needed.

prefer-array-flat.description: Prefers Array#flat() over legacy techniques to flatten arrays.

prefer-array-flat-map.description: Prefers the use of .flatMap() when map().flat() are used together.

prefer-array-index-of.description:
Enforces using indexOf or lastIndexOf instead of findIndex or findLastIndex
when the callback is a simple strict equality comparison.

prefer-array-some.description:
Prefers using Array#some() over Array#find(), Array#findLast() with comparing to undefined,
or Array#findIndex(), Array#findLastIndex()
and a non-zero length check on the result of Array#filter()

prefer-at.description: Prefer .at() method for index access and String#charAt().

prefer-bigint-literals.description:
Requires using BigInt literals (e.g. 123n) instead of calling the BigInt() constructor
with literal arguments such as numbers or numeric strings

prefer-blob-reading-methods.description: Recommends using Blob#text() and Blob#arrayBuffer() over FileReader#readAsText() and FileReader#readAsArrayBuffer().

prefer-class-fields.description: Prefers class field declarations over this assignments in constructors for static values.

prefer-classlist-toggle.description:
Prefers the use of element.classList.toggle(className, condition) over
conditional add/remove patterns.

prefer-code-point.description:
Prefers usage of String.prototype.codePointAt over String.prototype.charCodeAt.
Prefers usage of String.fromCodePoint over String.fromCharCode.

prefer-date-now.description: Prefers use of Date.now() over new Date().getTime() or new Date().valueOf().

prefer-default-parameters.description:
Instead of reassigning a function parameter, default parameters should be used. The foo = foo || 123 statement evaluates to 123 when foo is falsy, possibly leading to confusing behavior, whereas default parameters only apply when passed an undefined value.
This rule only reports reassignments to literal values.

prefer-dom-node-append.description: Enforces the use of, for example, document.body.append(div); over document.body.appendChild(div); for DOM nodes.

prefer-dom-node-dataset.description: Use .dataset on DOM elements over getAttribute(…), .setAttribute(…), .removeAttribute(…) and .hasAttribute(…).

prefer-dom-node-remove.description: Prefers the use of child.remove() over parentNode.removeChild(child).

prefer-dom-node-text-content.description: Enforces the use of .textContent over .innerText for DOM nodes.

prefer-global-this.description:
Enforces the use of globalThis instead of
environment‑specific global object aliases (window, self, or global).
Using the standard globalThis makes your code portable across browsers, Web Workers, Node.js,
and future JavaScript runtimes.

prefer-keyboard-event-key.description:
Enforces the use of KeyboardEvent#key over KeyboardEvent#keyCode which is deprecated.
The .key property is also more semantic and readable.

prefer-logical-operator-over-ternary.description: This rule finds ternary expressions that can be simplified to a logical operator.

prefer-math-min-max.description:
Prefers use of Math.min() and Math.max() instead of ternary
expressions when performing simple comparisons.

prefer-math-trunc.description: Prefers use of Math.trunc() instead of bitwise operations for clarity and more reliable results.

prefer-modern-math-apis.description: Checks for usage of legacy patterns for mathematical operations.

prefer-native-coercion-functions.description: Prefers built in functions, over custom ones with the same functionality.

prefer-negative-index.description: Prefer negative index over .length - index when possible

prefer-node-protocol.description: Prefer using the node:protocol when importing Node.js builtin modules

prefer-number-properties.description: Disallows use of parseInt(), parseFloat(), isNan(), isFinite(), Nan, Infinity and -Infinity as global variables.

prefer-object-from-entries.description:
Encourages using Object.fromEntries when converting an array of key-value pairs
into an object.

prefer-optional-catch-binding.description: Prefers omitting the catch binding parameter if it is unused

prefer-prototype-methods.description: This rule prefers borrowing methods from the prototype instead of the instance.

prefer-reflect-apply.description: Disallows the use of Function.prototype.apply() and suggests using Reflect.apply() instead.

prefer-regexp-test.description: Prefers RegExp#test() over String#match() and String#exec().

prefer-response-static-json.description: Enforces the use of Response.json() over new Response(JSON.stringify()).

prefer-set-has.description: Prefer Set#has() over Array#includes() when checking for existence or non-existence.

prefer-set-size.description: Prefer Set#size over Set#length when the Set is converted to an array.

prefer-string-raw.description: Prefers use of String.raw to avoid escaping .

prefer-string-replace-all.description: Prefers String#replaceAll() over String#replace() when using a regex with the global flag.

prefer-string-slice.description: Prefer String#slice() over String#substr() and String#substring().

prefer-string-trim-start-end.description: String#trimLeft() and String#trimRight() are aliases of String#trimStart() and String#trimEnd(). This is to ensure consistency and use direction-independent wording.

prefer-structured-clone.description: Prefer using structuredClone to create a deep clone.

prefer-top-level-await.description: Prefer top-level await over top-level promises and async function calls.

prefer-type-error.description: Enforce throwing a TypeError instead of a generic Error after a type checking if-statement.

require-array-join-separator.description: Enforce using the separator argument with Array#join()

require-module-attributes.description: This rule enforces non-empty attribute list in import/export statements and import() expressions.

require-module-specifiers.description: Enforce non-empty specifier list in import and export statements.

require-number-to-fixed-digits-argument.description: Enforce using the digits argument with Number.toFixed()

require-post-message-target-origin.description: Enforce using the targetOrigin argument with window.postMessage()

switch-case-braces.description:
Requires empty switch cases to omit braces, while non-empty cases must use braces.
This reduces visual clutter for empty cases and enforces proper scoping for non-empty ones.

text-encoding-identifier-case.description: This rule enforces consistent casing for text encoding identifiers, specifically:

throw-new-error.description: This rule makes sure you always use new when throwing an error.

consistent-each-for.description:
This rule ensure consistency on which method used to create parameterized test.
This configuration affects to different test function types (test, it, describe, suite).

consistent-test-filename.description:
This rule triggers an error when a file is considered a test file, but its name
does not match an expected filename format.

consistent-vitest-vi.description: This rule triggers an error when an unexpected vitest accessor is used.

hoisted-apis-on-top.description: Enforce hoisted APIs to be on top of the file.

no-conditional-tests.description:
The rule disallows the use of conditional statements within test cases to
ensure that tests are deterministic and clearly readable.

no-import-node-test.description:
This rule warns when node:test is imported (usually accidentally).
With --fix, it will replace the import with vitest.

no-importing-vitest-globals.description: The rule disallows import any vitest global function.

prefer-called-once.description:
Substitute toBeCalledTimes(1) and toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1) with
toBeCalledOnce() and toHaveBeenCalledOnce() respectively.

prefer-called-times.description: This rule aims to enforce the use of toBeCalledTimes(1) or toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1) over toBeCalledOnce() or toHaveBeenCalledOnce().

prefer-describe-function-title.description:
When testing a specific function, this rule aims to enforce passing a named function to describe()
instead of an equivalent hardcoded string.

prefer-expect-type-of.description: Enforce using expectTypeOf instead of expect(typeof ...)

prefer-import-in-mock.description: This rule enforces using a dynamic import() in vi.mock(), which improves type information and IntelliSense for the mocked module.

prefer-to-be-falsy.description:
This rule warns when toBe(false) is used with expect or expectTypeOf.
With --fix, it will be replaced with toBeFalsy().

prefer-to-be-object.description: This rule enforces using toBeObject() to check if a value is of type Object.

prefer-to-be-truthy.description:
This rule warns when toBe(true) is used with expect or expectTypeOf.
With --fix, it will be replaced with toBeTruthy().

require-local-test-context-for-concurren.description:
The rule is intended to ensure that concurrent snapshot tests are executed
within a properly configured local test context.

warn-todo.description:
This rule triggers warnings when .todo is used in describe, it, or test functions.
It is recommended to use this with your CI pipeline to annotate PR diffs.

define-emits-declaration.description:
This rule enforces defineEmits typing style which you should use type-based, strict type-literal (introduced in Vue 3.3), or runtime declaration.
This rule only works in setup script and lang="ts".

define-props-declaration.description:
This rule enforces defineProps typing style which you should use type-based or runtime declaration.
This rule only works in <script setup> with lang="ts".

define-props-destructuring.description:
This rule enforces a consistent style for handling Vue 3 Composition API props,
allowing you to choose between requiring destructuring or prohibiting it.

max-props.description: Enforce maximum number of props in Vue component.

no-arrow-functions-in-watch.description: This rule disallows using arrow functions when defining a watcher.

no-deprecated-destroyed-lifecycle.description: Disallow using deprecated destroyed and beforeDestroy lifecycle hooks in Vue.js 3.0.0+.

no-multiple-slot-args.description: Disallow passing multiple arguments to scoped slots.

no-required-prop-with-default.description: Enforce props with default values to be optional.

no-this-in-before-route-enter.description: Disallow this usage in a beforeRouteEnter method.

prefer-import-from-vue.description: Enforce import from 'vue' instead of import from '@vue/*'.

require-default-export.description: Require components to be the default export.

require-typed-ref.description: Require ref and shallowRef functions to be strongly typed.

valid-define-emits.description: This rule checks whether defineEmits compiler macro is valid.

valid-define-props.description: This rule checks whether defineProps compiler macro is valid.

typescript.name: typescript
typescript.description: ✨ Monorepo for all the tooling which enables ESLint to support TypeScript

adjacent-overload-signatures.description: Require that function overload signatures be consecutive

array-type.description: Require consistently using either `T[]` or `Array<T>` for arrays

await-thenable.description: Disallow awaiting a value that is not a Thenable

ban-ts-comment.description: Disallow `@ts-<directive>` comments or require descriptions after directives

class-literal-property-style.description: Enforce that literals on classes are exposed in a consistent style

consistent-generic-constructors.description: Enforce specifying generic type arguments on type annotation or constructor name of a constructor call

consistent-return.description: Require `return` statements to either always or never specify values

consistent-type-assertions.description: Enforce consistent usage of type assertions

consistent-type-definitions.description: Enforce type definitions to consistently use either `interface` or `type`

consistent-type-exports.description: Enforce consistent usage of type exports

consistent-type-imports.description: Enforce consistent usage of type imports

default-param-last.description: Enforce default parameters to be last

explicit-function-return-type.description: Require explicit return types on functions and class methods

explicit-member-accessibility.description: Require explicit accessibility modifiers on class properties and methods

explicit-module-boundary-types.description: Require explicit return and argument types on exported functions' and classes' public class methods

init-declarations.description: Require or disallow initialization in variable declarations

max-params.description: Enforce a maximum number of parameters in function definitions

member-ordering.description: Require a consistent member declaration order

method-signature-style.description: Enforce using a particular method signature syntax
