naming-convention.description: Enforce naming conventions for everything across a codebase

no-array-delete.description: Disallow using the `delete` operator on array values

no-base-to-string.description: Require `.toString()` and `.toLocaleString()` to only be called on objects which provide useful information when stringified

no-confusing-non-null-assertion.description: Disallow non-null assertion in locations that may be confusing

no-confusing-void-expression.description: Require expressions of type void to appear in statement position

no-duplicate-type-constituents.description: Disallow duplicate constituents of union or intersection types

no-dynamic-delete.description: Disallow using the `delete` operator on computed key expressions

no-empty-interface.description: Disallow the declaration of empty interfaces

no-empty-object-type.description: Disallow accidentally using the "empty object" type

no-floating-promises.description: Require Promise-like statements to be handled appropriately

no-for-in-array.description: Disallow iterating over an array with a for-in loop

no-import-type-side-effects.description: Enforce the use of top-level import type qualifier when an import only has specifiers with inline type qualifiers

no-inferrable-types.description: Disallow explicit type declarations for variables or parameters initialized to a number, string, or boolean

no-invalid-this.description: Disallow `this` keywords outside of classes or class-like objects

no-invalid-void-type.description: Disallow `void` type outside of generic or return types

no-loop-func.description: Disallow function declarations that contain unsafe references inside loop statements

no-loss-of-precision.description: Disallow literal numbers that lose precision

no-meaningless-void-operator.description: Disallow the `void` operator except when used to discard a value

no-misused-promises.description: Disallow Promises in places not designed to handle them

no-misused-spread.description: Disallow using the spread operator when it might cause unexpected behavior

no-mixed-enums.description: Disallow enums from having both number and string members

no-non-null-asserted-nullish-coalescing.description: Disallow non-null assertions in the left operand of a nullish coalescing operator

no-non-null-asserted-optional-chain.description: Disallow non-null assertions after an optional chain expression

no-non-null-assertion.description: Disallow non-null assertions using the `!` postfix operator

no-redundant-type-constituents.description: Disallow members of unions and intersections that do nothing or override type information

no-restricted-imports.description: Disallow specified modules when loaded by `import`

no-shadow.description: Disallow variable declarations from shadowing variables declared in the outer scope

no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare.description: Disallow unnecessary equality comparisons against boolean literals

no-unnecessary-condition.description: Disallow conditionals where the type is always truthy or always falsy

no-unnecessary-parameter-property-assign.description: Disallow unnecessary assignment of constructor property parameter

no-unnecessary-type-arguments.description: Disallow type arguments that are equal to the default

no-unnecessary-type-assertion.description: Disallow type assertions that do not change the type of an expression

no-unnecessary-type-constraint.description: Disallow unnecessary constraints on generic types

no-unnecessary-type-conversion.description: Disallow conversion idioms when they do not change the type or value of the expression

no-unnecessary-type-parameters.description: Disallow type parameters that aren't used multiple times

no-unsafe-argument.description: Disallow calling a function with a value with type `any`

no-unsafe-assignment.description: Disallow assigning a value with type `any` to variables and properties

no-unsafe-call.description: Disallow calling a value with type `any`

no-unsafe-enum-comparison.description: Disallow comparing an enum value with a non-enum value

no-unsafe-function-type.description: Disallow using the unsafe built-in Function type

no-unsafe-member-access.description: Disallow member access on a value with type `any`

no-unsafe-return.description: Disallow returning a value with type `any` from a function

no-unsafe-type-assertion.description: Disallow type assertions that narrow a type

no-unsafe-unary-minus.description: Require unary negation to take a number

no-use-before-define.description: Disallow the use of variables before they are defined

no-useless-default-assignment.description: Disallow default values that will never be used

no-useless-empty-export.description: Disallow empty exports that don't change anything in a module file

no-var-requires.description: Disallow `require` statements except in import statements

no-wrapper-object-types.description: Disallow using confusing built-in primitive class wrappers

non-nullable-type-assertion-style.description: Enforce non-null assertions over explicit type assertions

only-throw-error.description: Disallow throwing non-`Error` values as exceptions

parameter-properties.description: Require or disallow parameter properties in class constructors

prefer-as-const.description: Enforce the use of `as const` over literal type

prefer-destructuring.description: Require destructuring from arrays and/or objects

prefer-enum-initializers.description: Require each enum member value to be explicitly initialized

prefer-find.description: Enforce the use of Array.prototype.find() over Array.prototype.filter() followed by [0] when looking for a single result

prefer-for-of.description: Enforce the use of `for-of` loop over the standard `for` loop where possible

prefer-function-type.description: Enforce using function types instead of interfaces with call signatures

prefer-literal-enum-member.description: Require all enum members to be literal values

prefer-namespace-keyword.description: Require using `namespace` keyword over `module` keyword to declare custom TypeScript modules

prefer-nullish-coalescing.description: Enforce using the nullish coalescing operator instead of logical assignments or chaining

prefer-optional-chain.description: Enforce using concise optional chain expressions instead of chained logical ands, negated logical ors, or empty objects

prefer-promise-reject-errors.description: Require using Error objects as Promise rejection reasons

prefer-readonly.description: Require private members to be marked as `readonly` if they're never modified outside of the constructor

prefer-readonly-parameter-types.description: Require function parameters to be typed as `readonly` to prevent accidental mutation of inputs

prefer-reduce-type-parameter.description: Enforce using type parameter when calling `Array#reduce` instead of using a type assertion

prefer-regexp-exec.description: Enforce `RegExp#exec` over `String#match` if no global flag is provided

prefer-return-this-type.description: Enforce that `this` is used when only `this` type is returned

prefer-string-starts-ends-with.description: Enforce using `String#startsWith` and `String#endsWith` over other equivalent methods of checking substrings

promise-function-async.description: Require any function or method that returns a Promise to be marked async

related-getter-setter-pairs.description: Enforce that `get()` types should be assignable to their equivalent `set()` type

require-array-sort-compare.description: Require `Array#sort` and `Array#toSorted` calls to always provide a `compareFunction`

require-await.description: Disallow async functions which do not return promises and have no `await` expression

restrict-plus-operands.description: Require both operands of addition to be the same type and be `bigint`, `number`, or `string`

restrict-template-expressions.description: Enforce template literal expressions to be of `string` type

return-await.description: Enforce consistent awaiting of returned promises

sort-type-constituents.description: Enforce constituents of a type union/intersection to be sorted alphabetically

strict-boolean-expressions.description: Disallow certain types in boolean expressions

strict-void-return.description: Disallow passing a value-returning function in a position accepting a void function

switch-exhaustiveness-check.description: Require switch-case statements to be exhaustive

triple-slash-reference.description: Disallow certain triple slash directives in favor of ES6-style import declarations

typedef.description: Require type annotations in certain places

unbound-method.description: Enforce unbound methods are called with their expected scope

unified-signatures.description: Disallow two overloads that could be unified into one with a union or an optional/rest parameter

use-unknown-in-catch-callback-variable.description: Enforce typing arguments in Promise rejection callbacks as `unknown`

better-regex.description: Improve regexes by making them shorter, consistent, and safer.

catch-error-name.description: Enforce a specific parameter name in catch clauses.

consistent-date-clone.description: Prefer passing `Date` directly to the constructor when cloning.

consistent-empty-array-spread.description: Prefer consistent types when spreading a ternary in an array literal.

consistent-existence-index-check.description: Enforce consistent style for element existence checks with `indexOf()`, `lastIndexOf()`, `findIndex()`, and `findLastIndex()`.

consistent-function-scoping.description: Move function definitions to the highest possible scope.

error-message.description: Enforce passing a `message` value when creating a built-in error.

escape-case.description: Require escape sequences to use uppercase or lowercase values.

expiring-todo-comments.description: Add expiration conditions to TODO comments.

explicit-length-check.description: Enforce explicitly comparing the `length` or `size` property of a value.

filename-case.description: Enforce a case style for filenames.

import-style.description: Enforce specific import styles per module.

isolated-functions.description: Prevent usage of variables from outside the scope of isolated functions.

new-for-builtins.description: Enforce the use of `new` for all builtins, except `String`, `Number`, `Boolean`, `Symbol` and `BigInt`.

no-abusive-eslint-disable.description: Enforce specifying rules to disable in `eslint-disable` comments.

no-accessor-recursion.description: Disallow recursive access to `this` within getters and setters.

no-anonymous-default-export.description: Disallow anonymous functions and classes as the default export.

no-array-callback-reference.description: Prevent passing a function reference directly to iterator methods.

no-array-method-this-argument.description: Disallow using the `this` argument in array methods.

no-await-expression-member.description: Disallow member access from await expression.

no-console-spaces.description: Do not use leading/trailing space between `console.log` parameters.

no-for-loop.description: Do not use a `for` loop that can be replaced with a `for-of` loop.

no-hex-escape.description: Enforce the use of Unicode escapes instead of hexadecimal escapes.

no-immediate-mutation.description: Disallow immediate mutation after variable assignment.

no-invalid-fetch-options.description: Disallow invalid options in `fetch()` and `new Request()`.

no-invalid-remove-event-listener.description: Prevent calling `EventTarget#removeEventListener()` with the result of an expression.

no-lonely-if.description: Disallow `if` statements as the only statement in `if` blocks without `else`.

no-magic-array-flat-depth.description: Disallow a magic number as the `depth` argument in `Array#flat(…).`

no-named-default.description: Disallow named usage of default import and export.

no-negation-in-equality-check.description: Disallow negated expression in equality check.

no-new-buffer.description: Enforce the use of `Buffer.from()` and `Buffer.alloc()` instead of the deprecated `new Buffer()`.

no-null.description: Disallow the use of the `null` literal.

no-object-as-default-parameter.description: Disallow the use of objects as default parameters.

no-single-promise-in-promise-methods.description: Disallow passing single-element arrays to `Promise` methods.

no-static-only-class.description: Disallow classes that only have static members.

no-unnecessary-array-flat-depth.description: Disallow using `1` as the `depth` argument of `Array#flat()`.

no-unnecessary-array-splice-count.description: Disallow using `.length` or `Infinity` as the `deleteCount` or `skipCount` argument of `Array#{splice,toSpliced}()`.

no-unnecessary-polyfills.description: Enforce the use of built-in methods instead of unnecessary polyfills.

no-unnecessary-slice-end.description: Disallow using `.length` or `Infinity` as the `end` argument of `{Array,String,TypedArray}#slice()`.

no-useless-collection-argument.description: Disallow useless values or fallbacks in `Set`, `Map`, `WeakSet`, or `WeakMap`.

no-useless-fallback-in-spread.description: Disallow useless fallback when spreading in object literals.

no-useless-promise-resolve-reject.description: Disallow returning/yielding `Promise.resolve/reject()` in async functions or promise callbacks

no-useless-switch-case.description: Disallow useless case in switch statements.

no-zero-fractions.description: Disallow number literals with zero fractions or dangling dots.

number-literal-case.description: Enforce proper case for numeric literals.

numeric-separators-style.description: Enforce the style of numeric separators by correctly grouping digits.

prefer-array-find.description: Prefer `.find(…)` and `.findLast(…)` over the first or last element from `.filter(…)`.

prefer-array-flat.description: Prefer `Array#flat()` over legacy techniques to flatten arrays.

prefer-array-index-of.description: Prefer `Array#{indexOf,lastIndexOf}()` over `Array#{findIndex,findLastIndex}()` when looking for the index of an item.

prefer-at.description: Prefer `.at()` method for index access and `String#charAt()`.

prefer-class-fields.description: Prefer class field declarations over `this` assignments in constructors.

prefer-classlist-toggle.description: Prefer using `Element#classList.toggle()` to toggle class names.

prefer-date-now.description: Prefer `Date.now()` to get the number of milliseconds since the Unix Epoch.

prefer-dom-node-dataset.description: Prefer using `.dataset` on DOM elements over calling attribute methods.

prefer-import-meta-properties.description: Prefer `import.meta.{dirname,filename}` over legacy techniques for getting file paths.

prefer-includes.description: Prefer `.includes()` over `.indexOf()`, `.lastIndexOf()`, and `Array#some()` when checking for existence or non-existence.

prefer-json-parse-buffer.description: Prefer reading a JSON file as a buffer.

prefer-logical-operator-over-ternary.description: Prefer using a logical operator over a ternary.

prefer-math-min-max.description: Prefer `Math.min()` and `Math.max()` over ternaries for simple comparisons.

prefer-math-trunc.description: Enforce the use of `Math.trunc` instead of bitwise operators.

prefer-modern-dom-apis.description: Prefer `.before()` over `.insertBefore()`, `.replaceWith()` over `.replaceChild()`, prefer one of `.before()`, `.after()`, `.append()` or `.prepend()` over `insertAdjacentText()` and `insertAdjacentElement()`.

prefer-modern-math-apis.description: Prefer modern `Math` APIs over legacy patterns.

prefer-negative-index.description: Prefer negative index over `.length - index` when possible.

prefer-node-protocol.description: Prefer using the `node:` protocol when importing Node.js builtin modules.

prefer-number-properties.description: Prefer `Number` static properties over global ones.

prefer-object-from-entries.description: Prefer using `Object.fromEntries(…)` to transform a list of key-value pairs into an object.

prefer-prototype-methods.description: Prefer borrowing methods from the prototype instead of the instance.

prefer-set-has.description: Prefer `Set#has()` over `Array#includes()` when checking for existence or non-existence.

prefer-single-call.description: Enforce combining multiple `Array#push()`, `Element#classList.{add,remove}()`, and `importScripts()` into one call.

prefer-spread.description: Prefer the spread operator over `Array.from(…)`, `Array#concat(…)`, `Array#{slice,toSpliced}()` and `String#split('')`.

prefer-string-raw.description: Prefer using the `String.raw` tag to avoid escaping `\`.

prefer-string-replace-all.description: Prefer `String#replaceAll()` over regex searches with the global flag.

prefer-structured-clone.description: Prefer using `structuredClone` to create a deep clone.

prefer-ternary.description: Prefer ternary expressions over simple `if-else` statements.

prefer-top-level-await.description: Prefer top-level await over top-level promises and async function calls.

prefer-type-error.description: Enforce throwing `TypeError` in type checking conditions.

require-array-join-separator.description: Enforce using the separator argument with `Array#join()`.

require-module-attributes.description: Require non-empty module attributes for imports and exports

require-module-specifiers.description: Require non-empty specifier list in import and export statements.

require-number-to-fixed-digits-argument.description: Enforce using the digits argument with `Number#toFixed()`.

switch-case-braces.description: Enforce consistent brace style for `case` clauses.

text-encoding-identifier-case.description: Enforce consistent case for text encoding identifiers.

no-instanceof-array.description: Replaced by `unicorn/no-instanceof-builtins` which covers more cases.

no-length-as-slice-end.description: Replaced by `unicorn/no-unnecessary-slice-end` which covers more cases.

no-array-push-push.description: Replaced by `unicorn/prefer-single-call` which covers more cases.

boolean-prop-naming.description: Enforces consistent naming for boolean props

button-has-type.description: Disallow usage of `button` elements without an explicit `type` attribute

checked-requires-onchange-or-readonly.description: Enforce using `onChange` or `readonly` attribute when `checked` is used

default-props-match-prop-types.description: Enforce all defaultProps have a corresponding non-required PropType

destructuring-assignment.description: Enforce consistent usage of destructuring assignment of props, state, and context

display-name.description: Disallow missing displayName in a React component definition

forbid-dom-props.description: Disallow certain props on DOM Nodes

forward-ref-uses-ref.description: Require all forwardRef components include a ref parameter

function-component-definition.description: Enforce a specific function type for function components

hook-use-state.description: Ensure destructuring and symmetric naming of useState hook value and setter variables

iframe-missing-sandbox.description: Enforce sandbox attribute on iframe elements

jsx-boolean-value.description: Enforce boolean attributes notation in JSX

jsx-child-element-spacing.description: Enforce or disallow spaces inside of curly braces in JSX attributes and expressions

jsx-closing-bracket-location.description: Enforce closing bracket location in JSX

jsx-closing-tag-location.description: Enforce closing tag location for multiline JSX

jsx-curly-spacing.description: Enforce or disallow spaces inside of curly braces in JSX attributes and expressions

jsx-curly-newline.description: Enforce consistent linebreaks in curly braces in JSX attributes and expressions

jsx-equals-spacing.description: Enforce or disallow spaces around equal signs in JSX attributes

jsx-filename-extension.description: Disallow file extensions that may contain JSX

jsx-first-prop-new-line.description: Enforce proper position of the first property in JSX

jsx-handler-names.description: Enforce event handler naming conventions in JSX

jsx-key.description: Disallow missing `key` props in iterators/collection literals

jsx-max-props-per-line.description: Enforce maximum of props on a single line in JSX

jsx-newline.description: Require or prevent a new line after jsx elements and expressions.

jsx-no-bind.description: Disallow `.bind()` or arrow functions in JSX props

jsx-no-comment-textnodes.description: Disallow comments from being inserted as text nodes

jsx-no-constructed-context-values.description: Disallows JSX context provider values from taking values that will cause needless rerenders

jsx-no-leaked-render.description: Disallow problematic leaked values from being rendered

jsx-no-literals.description: Disallow usage of string literals in JSX

jsx-one-expression-per-line.description: Require one JSX element per line

jsx-curly-brace-presence.description: Disallow unnecessary JSX expressions when literals alone are sufficient or enforce JSX expressions on literals in JSX children or attributes

jsx-pascal-case.description: Enforce PascalCase for user-defined JSX components

jsx-fragments.description: Enforce shorthand or standard form for React fragments

jsx-props-no-multi-spaces.description: Disallow multiple spaces between inline JSX props

jsx-props-no-spread-multi.description: Disallow JSX prop spreading the same identifier multiple times

jsx-space-before-closing.description: Enforce spacing before closing bracket in JSX

jsx-tag-spacing.description: Enforce whitespace in and around the JSX opening and closing brackets

jsx-uses-react.description: Disallow React to be incorrectly marked as unused

jsx-uses-vars.description: Disallow variables used in JSX to be incorrectly marked as unused

jsx-wrap-multilines.description: Disallow missing parentheses around multiline JSX

no-access-state-in-setstate.description: Disallow when this.state is accessed within setState

no-adjacent-inline-elements.description: Disallow adjacent inline elements not separated by whitespace.

no-array-index-key.description: Disallow usage of Array index in keys

no-arrow-function-lifecycle.description: Lifecycle methods should be methods on the prototype, not class fields

no-children-prop.description: Disallow passing of children as props

no-danger.description: Disallow usage of dangerous JSX properties

no-danger-with-children.description: Disallow when a DOM element is using both children and dangerouslySetInnerHTML

no-did-mount-set-state.description: Disallow usage of setState in componentDidMount

no-did-update-set-state.description: Disallow usage of setState in componentDidUpdate

no-multi-comp.description: Disallow multiple component definition per file

no-namespace.description: Enforce that namespaces are not used in React elements

no-redundant-should-component-update.description: Disallow usage of shouldComponentUpdate when extending React.PureComponent

no-render-return-value.description: Disallow usage of the return value of ReactDOM.render

no-this-in-sfc.description: Disallow `this` from being used in stateless functional components

no-unescaped-entities.description: Disallow unescaped HTML entities from appearing in markup

no-unknown-property.description: Disallow usage of unknown DOM property

no-unsafe.description: Disallow usage of unsafe lifecycle methods

no-unstable-nested-components.description: Disallow creating unstable components inside components

no-unused-class-component-methods.description: Disallow declaring unused methods of component class

no-object-type-as-default-prop.description: Disallow usage of referential-type variables as default param in functional component

no-will-update-set-state.description: Disallow usage of setState in componentWillUpdate

prefer-es6-class.description: Enforce ES5 or ES6 class for React Components

prefer-stateless-function.description: Enforce stateless components to be written as a pure function

prop-types.description: Disallow missing props validation in a React component definition

react-in-jsx-scope.description: Disallow missing React when using JSX

require-default-props.description: Enforce a defaultProps definition for every prop that is not a required prop

require-optimization.description: Enforce React components to have a shouldComponentUpdate method

require-render-return.description: Enforce ES5 or ES6 class for returning value in render function

self-closing-comp.description: Disallow extra closing tags for components without children

state-in-constructor.description: Enforce class component state initialization style

static-property-placement.description: Enforces where React component static properties should be positioned.

style-prop-object.description: Enforce style prop value is an object

void-dom-elements-no-children.description:
Disallow void DOM elements (e.g. ``, `
`) from receiving children

react-perf.name: react-perf
react-perf.description: Performance-minded React linting rules for ESLint

jsx-no-jsx-as-prop.description: Prevent JSX as JSX prop value

react-hooks.name: react-hooks
react-hooks.description: This plugin helps you catch violations of React’s rules at build time, ensuring your components and hooks follow React’s rules for correctness and performance

exhaustive-deps.description: verifies the list of dependencies for Hooks like useEffect and similar

capitalized-calls.description: Validates against calling capitalized functions/methods instead of using JSX

static-components.description: Validates that components are static, not recreated every render. Components that are recreated dynamically can reset state and trigger excessive re-rendering

use-memo.description: Validates usage of the useMemo() hook against common mistakes. See [`useMemo()` docs](https://react.dev/reference/react/useMemo) for more information.

void-use-memo.description: Validates that useMemos always return a value and that the result of the useMemo is used by the component/hook. See [`useMemo()` docs](https://react.dev/reference/react/useMemo) for more information.

component-hook-factories.description: Validates against higher order functions defining nested components or hooks. Components and hooks should be defined at the module level

preserve-manual-memoization.description: Validates that existing manual memoized is preserved by the compiler. React Compiler will only compile components and hooks if its inference [matches or exceeds the existing manual memoization](https://react.dev/learn/react-compiler/introduction#what-should-i-do-about-usememo-usecallback-and-reactmemo)

incompatible-library.description: Validates against usage of libraries which are incompatible with memoization (manual or automatic)

immutability.description: Validates against mutating props, state, and other values that [are immutable](https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure#props-and-state-are-immutable)

globals.description: Validates against assignment/mutation of globals during render, part of ensuring that [side effects must render outside of render](https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure#side-effects-must-run-outside-of-render)

refs.description: Validates correct usage of refs, not reading/writing during render. See the "pitfalls" section in [`useRef()` usage](https://react.dev/reference/react/useRef#usage)

memoized-effect-dependencies.description: Validates that effect dependencies are memoized

set-state-in-effect.description: Validates against calling setState synchronously in an effect, which can lead to re-renders that degrade performance

no-deriving-state-in-effects.description: Validates against deriving values from state in an effect

error-boundaries.description: Validates usage of error boundaries instead of try/catch for errors in child components

purity.description: Validates that [components/hooks are pure](https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure) by checking that they do not call known-impure functions

set-state-in-render.description: Validates against setting state during render, which can trigger additional renders and potential infinite render loops

unsupported-syntax.description: Validates against syntax that we do not plan to support in React Compiler

rule-suppression.description: Validates against suppression of other rules

automatic-effect-dependencies.description: Verifies that automatic effect dependencies are compiled if opted-in

next.name: next
next.description: Base configuration with Next.js, React, and React Hooks rules.

google-font-display.description: Enforce font-display behavior with Google Fonts.

google-font-preconnect.description: Ensure `preconnect` is used with Google Fonts.

inline-script-id.description: Enforce `id` attribute on `next/script` components with inline content.

next-script-for-ga.description: Prefer `@next/third-parties/google` when using the inline script for Google Analytics and Tag Manager.

no-async-client-component.description: Prevent Client Components from being async functions.

no-before-interactive-script-outside-doc.description: Prevent usage of `next/script`'s `beforeInteractive` strategy outside of `pages/_document.js`.

no-html-link-for-pages.description: Prevent usage of `<a>` elements to navigate to internal Next.js pages.

no-img-element.description: Prevent usage of `` element due to slower LCP and higher bandwidth.

no-title-in-document-head.description: Prevent usage of `<title>` with `Head` component from `next/document`.

no-typos.description: Prevent common typos in Next.js data fetching functions.

eslint-plugin-import.name: eslint-plugin-import
eslint-plugin-import.description: ESLint plugin with rules that help validate proper imports.

no-unresolved.description: Ensure imports point to a file/module that can be resolved.

named.description: Ensure named imports correspond to a named export in the remote file.

default.description: Ensure a default export is present, given a default import.

namespace.description: Ensure imported namespaces contain dereferenced properties as they are dereferenced.

export.description: Forbid any invalid exports, i.e. re-export of the same name.

no-mutable-exports.description: Forbid the use of mutable exports with `var` or `let`.

extensions.description: Ensure consistent use of file extension within the import path.

no-restricted-paths.description: Enforce which files can be imported in a given folder.

no-internal-modules.description: Forbid importing the submodules of other modules.

group-exports.description: Prefer named exports to be grouped together in a single export declaration

no-relative-packages.description: Forbid importing packages through relative paths.

no-relative-parent-imports.description: Forbid importing modules from parent directories.

consistent-type-specifier-style.description: Enforce or ban the use of inline type-only markers for named imports.

no-self-import.description: Forbid a module from importing itself.

no-cycle.description: Forbid a module from importing a module with a dependency path back to itself.

no-named-as-default.description: Forbid use of exported name as identifier of default export.

no-named-as-default-member.description: Forbid use of exported name as property of default export.

no-anonymous-default-export.description: Forbid anonymous values as default exports.

no-unused-modules.description: Forbid modules without exports, or exports without matching import in another module.

no-duplicates.description: Forbid repeated import of the same module in multiple places.

first.description: Ensure all imports appear before other statements.

max-dependencies.description: Enforce the maximum number of dependencies a module can have.

no-extraneous-dependencies.description: Forbid the use of extraneous packages.

no-absolute-path.description: Forbid import of modules using absolute paths.

no-webpack-loader-syntax.description: Forbid webpack loader syntax in imports.

order.description: Enforce a convention in module import order.

newline-after-import.description: Enforce a newline after import statements.

prefer-default-export.description: Prefer a default export if module exports a single name or multiple names.

unambiguous.description: Forbid potentially ambiguous parse goal (`script` vs. `module`).

no-useless-path-segments.description: Forbid unnecessary path segments in import and require statements.

dynamic-import-chunkname.description: Enforce a leading comment with the webpackChunkName for dynamic imports.

no-import-module-exports.description: Forbid import statements with CommonJS module.exports.

enforce-node-protocol-usage.description: Enforce either using, or omitting, the `node:` protocol when importing Node.js builtin modules.

exports-last.description: Ensure all exports appear after other statements.

no-deprecated.description: Forbid imported names marked with `@deprecated` documentation tag.

jsdoc.name: jsdoc
jsdoc.description: JSDoc specific linting rules for ESLint.

check-access.description: Checks that `@access` tags have a valid value.

check-alignment.description: Reports invalid alignment of JSDoc block asterisks.

check-examples.description: @deprecated - Use `getJsdocProcessorPlugin` processor; ensures that (JavaScript) samples within `@example` tags adhere to ESLint rules.

check-indentation.description: Reports invalid padding inside JSDoc blocks.

check-line-alignment.description: Reports invalid alignment of JSDoc block lines.

check-param-names.description: Checks for dupe `@param` names, that nested param names have roots, and that parameter names in function declarations match JSDoc param names.

check-property-names.description: Ensures that property names in JSDoc are not duplicated on the same block and that nested properties have defined roots.

check-syntax.description: Reports against syntax not valid for the mode (e.g., Google Closure Compiler in non-Closure mode).

check-template-names.description: Checks that any `@template` names are actually used in the connected `@typedef` or type alias.

check-types.description: Reports types deemed invalid (customizable and with defaults, for preventing and/or recommending replacements).

check-values.description: This rule checks the values for a handful of tags: `@version`, `@since`, `@license` and `@author`.

convert-to-jsdoc-comments.description: Converts non-JSDoc comments preceding or following nodes into JSDoc ones

empty-tags.description: Checks tags that are expected to be empty (e.g., `@abstract` or `@async`), reporting if they have content

escape-inline-tags.description: Reports use of JSDoc tags in non-tag positions (in the default "typescript" mode).

implements-on-classes.description: Prohibits use of `@implements` on non-constructor functions (to enforce the tag only being used on classes/constructors).

imports-as-dependencies.description: Reports if JSDoc `import()` statements point to a package which is not listed in `dependencies` or `devDependencies`

informative-docs.description: This rule reports doc comments that only restate their attached name.

lines-before-block.description: Enforces minimum number of newlines before JSDoc comment blocks

match-description.description: Enforces a regular expression pattern on descriptions.

match-name.description: Reports the name portion of a JSDoc tag if matching or not matching a given regular expression.

multiline-blocks.description: Controls how and whether JSDoc blocks can be expressed as single or multiple line blocks.

no-bad-blocks.description: This rule checks for multi-line-style comments which fail to meet the criteria of a JSDoc block.

no-blank-block-descriptions.description: If tags are present, this rule will prevent empty lines in the block description. If no tags are present, this rule will prevent extra empty lines in the block description.

no-blank-blocks.description: Removes empty blocks with nothing but possibly line breaks

no-defaults.description: This rule reports defaults being used on the relevant portion of `@param` or `@default`.

no-missing-syntax.description: Reports when certain comment structures are always expected.

no-multi-asterisks.description: Prevents use of multiple asterisks at the beginning of lines.

no-restricted-syntax.description: Reports when certain comment structures are present.

no-types.description: This rule reports types being used on `@param` or `@returns` (redundant with TypeScript).

no-undefined-types.description: Besides some expected built-in types, prohibits any types not specified as globals or within `@typedef`.

require-asterisk-prefix.description: Requires that each JSDoc line starts with an `*`.

require-description.description: Requires that all functions (and potentially other contexts) have a description.

require-description-complete-sentence.description: Requires that block description, explicit `@description`, and `@param`/`@returns` tag descriptions are written in complete sentences.

require-example.description: Requires that all functions (and potentially other contexts) have examples.

require-file-overview.description: Checks that all files have one `@file`, `@fileoverview`, or `@overview` tag at the beginning of the file.

require-hyphen-before-param-description.description: Requires a hyphen before the `@param` description (and optionally before `@property` descriptions).

require-jsdoc.description: Checks for presence of JSDoc comments, on functions and potentially other contexts (optionally limited to exports).

require-param.description: Requires that all function parameters are documented with a `@param` tag.

require-param-description.description: Requires that each `@param` tag has a `description` value.

require-param-name.description: Requires that all `@param` tags have names.

require-param-type.description: Requires that each `@param` tag has a type value (in curly brackets).

require-property.description: Requires that all `@typedef` and `@namespace` tags have `@property` when their type is a plain `object`, `Object`, or `PlainObject`.

require-property-description.description: Requires that each `@property` tag has a `description` value.

require-property-name.description: Requires that all `@property` tags have names.

require-property-type.description: Requires that each `@property` tag has a type value (in curly brackets).

require-rejects.description: Requires that Promise rejections are documented with `@rejects` tags.

require-returns.description: Requires that returns are documented with `@returns`.

require-returns-check.description: Requires a return statement in function body if a `@returns` tag is specified in JSDoc comment(and reports if multiple `@returns` tags are present).

require-returns-description.description: Requires that the `@returns` tag has a `description` value (not including `void`/`undefined` type returns).

require-returns-type.description: Requires that `@returns` tag has type value (in curly brackets).

require-tags.description: Requires tags be present, optionally for specific contexts

require-template.description: Requires `@template` tags be present when type parameters are used.

require-throws.description: Requires that throw statements are documented with `@throws` tags.

require-yields.description: Requires yields are documented with `@yields` tags.

require-yields-check.description: Ensures that if a `@yields` is present that a `yield` (or `yield` with a value) is present in the function body (or that if a `@next` is present that there is a yield with a return value present).

sort-tags.description: Sorts tags by a specified sequence according to tag name, optionally adding line breaks between tag groups.

tag-lines.description: Enforces lines (or no lines) before, after, or between tags.

text-escaping.description: Auto-escape certain characters that are input within block and tag descriptions.

ts-method-signature-style.description: Prefers either function properties or method signatures

ts-no-empty-object-type.description: Warns against use of the empty object type

ts-no-unnecessary-template-expression.description: Catches unnecessary template expressions such as string expressions within a template literal.

ts-prefer-function-type.description: Prefers function types over call signatures when there are no other properties.

valid-types.description: Requires all types/namepaths to be valid JSDoc, Closure compiler, or TypeScript types (configurable in settings).

eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y.name: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y
eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y.description: Static AST checker for a11y rules on JSX elements.

accessible-emoji.description: Enforce emojis are wrapped in `<span>` and provide screen reader access.

alt-text.description: Enforce all elements that require alternative text have meaningful information to relay back to end user.

anchor-ambiguous-text.description: Enforce `<a>` text to not exactly match "click here", "here", "link", or "a link".

anchor-has-content.description: Enforce all anchors to contain accessible content.

anchor-is-valid.description: Enforce all anchors are valid, navigable elements.

aria-activedescendant-has-tabindex.description: Enforce elements with aria-activedescendant are tabbable.

aria-proptypes.description: Enforce ARIA state and property values are valid.

aria-role.description: Enforce that elements with ARIA roles must use a valid, non-abstract ARIA role.

aria-unsupported-elements.description: Enforce that elements that do not support ARIA roles, states, and properties do not have those attributes.

autocomplete-valid.description: Enforce that autocomplete attributes are used correctly.

click-events-have-key-events.description: Enforce a clickable non-interactive element has at least one keyboard event listener.

control-has-associated-label.description: Enforce that a control (an interactive element) has a text label.

heading-has-content.description: Enforce heading (`h1`, `h2`, etc) elements contain accessible content.

iframe-has-title.description: Enforce iframe elements have a title attribute.

img-redundant-alt.description: Enforce `` alt prop does not contain the word "image", "picture", or "photo".

interactive-supports-focus.description: Enforce that elements with interactive handlers like `onClick` must be focusable.

label-has-associated-control.description: Enforce that a `label` tag has a text label and an associated control.

label-has-for.description: Enforce that `<label>` elements have the `htmlFor` prop.

lang.description: Enforce lang attribute has a valid value.

media-has-caption.description: Enforces that `<audio>` and `<video>` elements must have a `<track>` for captions.

mouse-events-have-key-events.description: Enforce that `onMouseOver`/`onMouseOut` are accompanied by `onFocus`/`onBlur` for keyboard-only users.

no-access-key.description: Enforce that the `accessKey` prop is not used on any element to avoid complications with keyboard commands used by a screen reader.

no-aria-hidden-on-focusable.description: Disallow `aria-hidden="true"` from being set on focusable elements.

no-autofocus.description: Enforce autoFocus prop is not used.

no-distracting-elements.description: Enforce distracting elements are not used.

no-interactive-element-to-noninteractive.description: Interactive elements should not be assigned non-interactive roles.

no-noninteractive-element-interactions.description: Non-interactive elements should not be assigned mouse or keyboard event listeners.

no-noninteractive-element-to-interactive.description: Non-interactive elements should not be assigned interactive roles.

no-noninteractive-tabindex.description: `tabIndex` should only be declared on interactive elements.

no-onchange.description: Enforce usage of `onBlur` over `onChange` on select menus for accessibility.

no-redundant-roles.description: Enforce explicit role property is not the same as implicit/default role property on element.

no-static-element-interactions.description: Enforce that non-interactive, visible elements (such as `<div>`) that have click handlers use the role attribute.

prefer-tag-over-role.description: Enforces using semantic DOM elements over the ARIA `role` property.

role-has-required-aria-props.description: Enforce that elements with ARIA roles must have all required attributes for that role.

role-supports-aria-props.description: Enforce that elements with explicit or implicit roles defined contain only `aria-*` properties supported by that `role`.

scope.description: Enforce `scope` prop is only used on `<th>` elements.

tabindex-no-positive.description: Enforce `tabIndex` value is not greater than zero.

file-extension-in-import.description: enforce the style of file extensions in `import` declarations

global-require.description: require `require()` calls to be placed at top-level module scope

no-callback-literal.description: enforce Node.js-style error-first callback pattern is followed

no-extraneous-import.description: disallow `import` declarations which import extraneous modules

no-extraneous-require.description: disallow `require()` expressions which import extraneous modules

no-missing-import.description: disallow `import` declarations which import missing modules

no-missing-require.description: disallow `require()` expressions which import missing modules

no-mixed-requires.description: disallow `require` calls to be mixed with regular variable declarations

no-restricted-import.description: disallow specified modules when loaded by `import` declarations

no-restricted-require.description: disallow specified modules when loaded by `require`

no-unpublished-import.description: disallow `import` declarations which import private modules

no-unpublished-require.description: disallow `require()` expressions which import private modules

no-unsupported-features/es-builtins.description: disallow unsupported ECMAScript built-ins on the specified version

no-unsupported-features/es-syntax.description: disallow unsupported ECMAScript syntax on the specified version

no-unsupported-features/node-builtins.description: disallow unsupported Node.js built-in APIs on the specified version

prefer-global/timers.description: enforce either global timer functions or `require("timers")`

prefer-node-protocol.description: enforce using the `node:` protocol when importing Node.js builtin modules.

process-exit-as-throw.description: require that `process.exit()` expressions use the same code path as `throw`

no-hide-core-modules.description: disallow third-party modules which are hiding core modules

promise.name: promise
promise.description: Enforce best practices for JavaScript promises

param-names.description: Enforce consistent param names and ordering when creating new promises.

no-return-wrap.description: Disallow wrapping values in `Promise.resolve` or `Promise.reject` when not needed.

always-return.description: Require returning inside each `then()` to create readable and reusable Promise chains.

catch-or-return.description: Enforce the use of `catch()` on un-returned promises.

prefer-await-to-then.description: Prefer `await` to `then()`/`catch()`/`finally()` for reading Promise values.

prefer-catch.description: Prefer `catch` to `then(a, b)`/`then(null, b)` for handling errors.

no-native.description: Require creating a `Promise` constructor before using it in an ES5 environment.

no-callback-in-promise.description: Disallow calling `cb()` inside of a `then()` (use [util.callbackify][] instead).

no-promise-in-callback.description: Disallow using promises inside of callbacks.

avoid-new.description: Disallow creating `new` promises outside of utility libs (use [util.promisify][] instead).

no-new-statics.description: Disallow calling `new` on a Promise static method.

valid-params.description: Enforces the proper number of arguments are passed to Promise functions.

no-multiple-resolved.description: Disallow creating new promises with paths that resolve multiple times.

spec-only.description: Disallow use of non-standard Promise static methods.

expect-expect.description: Enforce assertion to be made in a test body

max-expects.description: Enforces a maximum number assertion calls in a test body

max-nested-describe.description: Enforces a maximum depth to nested describe calls

no-done-callback.description: Disallow using a callback in asynchronous tests and hooks

no-duplicate-hooks.description: Disallow duplicate setup and teardown hooks

no-error-equal.description: Disallow using equality matchers on error types

no-export.description: Disallow using `exports` in files containing tests

no-standalone-expect.description: Disallow using `expect` outside of `it` or `test` blocks

no-unnecessary-assertion.description: Disallow unnecessary assertions based on types

no-unneeded-async-expect-function.description: Disallow unnecessary async function wrapper for expected promises

no-untyped-mock-factory.description: Disallow using `jest.mock()` factories without an explicit type parameter

prefer-comparison-matcher.description: Suggest using the built-in comparison matchers

prefer-each.description: Prefer using `.each` rather than manual loops

prefer-ending-with-an-expect.description: Prefer having the last statement in a test be an assertion

prefer-equality-matcher.description: Suggest using the built-in equality matchers

prefer-hooks-in-order.description: Prefer having hooks in a consistent order

prefer-hooks-on-top.description: Suggest having hooks before any test cases

prefer-mock-promise-shorthand.description: Prefer mock resolved/rejected shorthands for promises

prefer-snapshot-hint.description: Prefer including a hint with external snapshots

require-hook.description: Require setup and teardown code to be within a hook

require-top-level-describe.description: Require test cases and hooks to be inside a `describe` block

unbound-method.description: Enforce unbound methods are called with their expected scope

valid-expect-in-promise.description: Require promises that have expectations in their chain to be valid

valid-expect-with-promise.description: Require that `resolve` and `reject` modifiers are present (and only) for promise-like types

valid-mock-module-path.description: Disallow mocking of non-existing module paths

max-nested-describe.description: require describe block to be less than set max value or default value

expect-expect.description: enforce having expectation in test body

consistent-test-it.description: enforce using test or it but not both

max-expects.description: enforce a maximum number of expect per test

no-done-callback.description: disallow using a callback in asynchronous tests and hooks

no-duplicate-hooks.description: disallow duplicate hooks and teardown hooks

no-restricted-matchers.description: disallow the use of certain matchers

no-standalone-expect.description: disallow using `expect` outside of `it` or `test` blocks

prefer-equality-matcher.description: enforce using the built-in quality matchers

prefer-each.description: enforce using `each` rather than manual loops

prefer-hooks-on-top.description: enforce having hooks before any test cases

prefer-hooks-in-order.description: enforce having hooks in consistent order

require-local-test-context-for-concurren.description: require local Test Context for concurrent snapshot tests

prefer-mock-promise-shorthand.description: enforce mock resolved/rejected shorthands for promises

prefer-snapshot-hint.description: enforce including a hint with external snapshots

require-top-level-describe.description: enforce that all tests are in a top-level describe

require-to-throw-message.description: require toThrow() to be called with an error message

require-hook.description: require setup and teardown to be within a hook

prefer-comparison-matcher.description: enforce using the built-in comparison matchers

prefer-expect-assertions.description: enforce using expect assertions instead of callbacks

array-bracket-newline.description: Enforce linebreaks after opening and before closing array brackets in `<template>`

array-bracket-spacing.description: Enforce consistent spacing inside array brackets in `<template>`

array-element-newline.description: Enforce line breaks after each array element in `<template>`

arrow-spacing.description: Enforce consistent spacing before and after the arrow in arrow functions in `<template>`

attribute-hyphenation.description: enforce attribute naming style on custom components in template

block-order.description: enforce order of component top-level elements

block-spacing.description: Disallow or enforce spaces inside of blocks after opening block and before closing block in `<template>`

block-tag-newline.description: enforce line breaks after opening and before closing block-level tags

brace-style.description: Enforce consistent brace style for blocks in `<template>`

comma-dangle.description: Require or disallow trailing commas in `<template>`

comma-spacing.description: Enforce consistent spacing before and after commas in `<template>`

component-definition-name-casing.description: enforce specific casing for component definition name

component-name-in-template-casing.description: enforce specific casing for the component naming style in template

component-options-name-casing.description: enforce the casing of component name in `components` options

custom-event-name-casing.description: enforce specific casing for custom event name

define-macros-order.description: enforce order of compiler macros (`defineProps`, `defineEmits`, etc.)

define-props-destructuring.description: enforce consistent style for props destructuring

dot-location.description: Enforce consistent newlines before and after dots in `<template>`

dot-notation.description: Enforce dot notation whenever possible in `<template>`

enforce-style-attribute.description: enforce or forbid the use of the `scoped` and `module` attributes in SFC top level style tags

eqeqeq.description: Require the use of `===` and `!==` in `<template>`

first-attribute-linebreak.description: enforce the location of first attribute

func-call-spacing.description: Require or disallow spacing between function identifiers and their invocations in `<template>`

html-button-has-type.description: disallow usage of button without an explicit type attribute

html-closing-bracket-newline.description: require or disallow a line break before tag's closing brackets

html-closing-bracket-spacing.description: require or disallow a space before tag's closing brackets

html-comment-content-newline.description: enforce unified line break in HTML comments

html-comment-content-spacing.description: enforce unified spacing in HTML comments

html-comment-indent.description: enforce consistent indentation in HTML comments

html-quotes.description: enforce quotes style of HTML attributes

jsx-uses-vars.description: prevent variables used in JSX to be marked as unused

key-spacing.description: Enforce consistent spacing between property names and type annotations in types and interfaces in `<template>`

keyword-spacing.description: Enforce consistent spacing before and after keywords in `<template>`

match-component-file-name.description: require component name property to match its file name

match-component-import-name.description: require the registered component name to match the imported component name

max-attributes-per-line.description: enforce the maximum number of attributes per line

max-len.description: enforce a maximum line length in `.vue` files

max-lines-per-block.description: enforce maximum number of lines in Vue SFC blocks

max-props.description: enforce maximum number of props in Vue component

multi-word-component-names.description: require component names to be always multi-word

multiline-html-element-content-newline.description: require a line break before and after the contents of a multiline element

multiline-ternary.description: Enforce newlines between operands of ternary expressions in `<template>`

mustache-interpolation-spacing.description: enforce unified spacing in mustache interpolations

new-line-between-multi-line-property.description: enforce new lines between multi-line properties in Vue components

next-tick-style.description: enforce Promise or callback style in `nextTick`

no-arrow-functions-in-watch.description: disallow using arrow functions to define watcher

no-async-in-computed-properties.description: disallow asynchronous actions in computed properties

no-bare-strings-in-template.description: disallow the use of bare strings in `<template>`

no-child-content.description: disallow element's child contents which would be overwritten by a directive like `v-html` or `v-text`

no-constant-condition.description: Disallow constant expressions in conditions in `<template>`

no-custom-modifiers-on-v-model.description: disallow custom modifiers on v-model used on the component

no-deprecated-data-object-declaration.description: disallow using deprecated object declaration on data (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-destroyed-lifecycle.description: disallow using deprecated `destroyed` and `beforeDestroy` lifecycle hooks (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-events-api.description: disallow using deprecated events api (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-filter.description: disallow using deprecated filters syntax (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-functional-template.description: disallow using deprecated the `functional` template (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-html-element-is.description: disallow using deprecated the `is` attribute on HTML elements (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-props-default-this.description: disallow deprecated `this` access in props default function (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-router-link-tag-prop.description: disallow using deprecated `tag` property on `RouterLink` (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-v-bind-sync.description: disallow use of deprecated `.sync` modifier on `v-bind` directive (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-deprecated-v-on-number-modifiers.description: disallow using deprecated number (keycode) modifiers (in Vue.js 3.0.0+)

no-duplicate-attr-inheritance.description: enforce `inheritAttrs` to be set to `false` when using `v-bind="$attrs"`

no-duplicate-class-names.description: disallow duplication of class names in class attributes

no-empty-component-block.description: disallow the `<template>` `<script>` `<style>` block to be empty

no-literals-in-template.description: disallow object, array, and function literals in template

no-loss-of-precision.description: Disallow literal numbers that lose precision in `<template>`

no-multiple-objects-in-class.description: disallow passing multiple objects in an array to class

no-multiple-slot-args.description: disallow passing multiple arguments to scoped slots

no-multiple-template-root.description: disallow adding multiple root nodes to the template

no-potential-component-option-typo.description: disallow a potential typo in your component property

no-ref-as-operand.description: disallow use of value wrapped by `ref()` (Composition API) as an operand

no-ref-object-reactivity-loss.description: disallow usages of ref objects that can lead to loss of reactivity

no-required-prop-with-default.description: enforce props with default values to be optional

no-reserved-component-names.description: disallow the use of reserved names in component definitions

no-restricted-class.description: disallow specific classes in Vue components

no-setup-props-reactivity-loss.description: disallow usages that lose the reactivity of `props` passed to `setup`

no-shared-component-data.description: enforce component's data property to be a function

no-side-effects-in-computed-properties.description: disallow side effects in computed properties

no-spaces-around-equal-signs-in-attribut.description: disallow spaces around equal signs in attribute

no-template-shadow.description: disallow variable declarations from shadowing variables declared in the outer scope

no-template-target-blank.description: disallow target="_blank" attribute without rel="noopener noreferrer"

no-undef-components.description: disallow use of undefined components in `<template>`

no-undef-directives.description: disallow use of undefined custom directives

no-unsupported-features.description: disallow unsupported Vue.js syntax on the specified version

no-unused-components.description: disallow registering components that are not used inside templates

no-unused-vars.description: disallow unused variable definitions of v-for directives or scope attributes

no-use-computed-property-like-method.description: disallow use computed property like method

no-use-v-else-with-v-for.description: disallow using `v-else-if`/`v-else` on the same element as `v-for`

no-use-v-if-with-v-for.description: disallow using `v-if` on the same element as `v-for`

no-useless-concat.description: Disallow unnecessary concatenation of literals or template literals in `<template>`

no-v-for-template-key-on-child.description: disallow key of `<template v-for>` placed on child elements

no-v-html.description: disallow use of v-html to prevent XSS attack

no-v-model-argument.description: disallow adding an argument to `v-model` used in custom component

object-curly-newline.description: Enforce consistent line breaks after opening and before closing braces in `<template>`

object-curly-spacing.description: Enforce consistent spacing inside braces in `<template>`

object-property-newline.description: Enforce placing object properties on separate lines in `<template>`

object-shorthand.description: Require or disallow method and property shorthand syntax for object literals in `<template>`

one-component-per-file.description: enforce that each component should be in its own file

operator-linebreak.description: Enforce consistent linebreak style for operators in `<template>`

order-in-components.description: enforce order of properties in components

padding-line-between-blocks.description: require or disallow padding lines between blocks

padding-line-between-tags.description: require or disallow newlines between sibling tags in template

padding-lines-in-component-definition.description: require or disallow padding lines in component definition

prefer-define-options.description: enforce use of `defineOptions` instead of default export

prefer-import-from-vue.description: enforce import from 'vue' instead of import from '@vue/*'

prefer-prop-type-boolean-first.description: enforce `Boolean` comes first in component prop types

prefer-separate-static-class.description: require static class names in template to be in a separate `class` attribute

prefer-template.description: Require template literals instead of string concatenation in `<template>`

prefer-true-attribute-shorthand.description: require shorthand form attribute when `v-bind` value is `true`

prefer-use-template-ref.description: require using `useTemplateRef` instead of `ref`/`shallowRef` for template refs

prop-name-casing.description: enforce specific casing for the Prop name in Vue components

quote-props.description: Require quotes around object literal, type literal, interfaces and enums property names in `<template>`

require-default-export.description: require components to be the default export

require-direct-export.description: require the component to be directly exported

require-explicit-emits.description: require `emits` option with name triggered by `$emit()`

require-explicit-slots.description: require slots to be explicitly defined

require-macro-variable-name.description: require a certain macro variable name

require-name-property.description: require a name property in Vue components

require-prop-comment.description: require props to have a comment

require-prop-type-constructor.description: require prop type to be a constructor

require-render-return.description: enforce render function to always return value

require-slots-as-functions.description: enforce properties of `$slots` to be used as a function

require-toggle-inside-transition.description: require control the display of the content inside `<transition>`

require-typed-object-prop.description: enforce adding type declarations to object props

require-typed-ref.description: require `ref` and `shallowRef` functions to be strongly typed

require-valid-default-prop.description: enforce props default values to be valid

restricted-component-names.description: enforce using only specific component names

return-in-computed-property.description: enforce that a return statement is present in computed property

return-in-emits-validator.description: enforce that a return statement is present in emits validator

singleline-html-element-content-newline.description: require a line break before and after the contents of a singleline element

slot-name-casing.description: enforce specific casing for slot names

sort-keys.description: enforce sort-keys in a manner that is compatible with order-in-components

space-in-parens.description: Enforce consistent spacing inside parentheses in `<template>`

space-infix-ops.description: Require spacing around infix operators in `<template>`

space-unary-ops.description: Enforce consistent spacing before or after unary operators in `<template>`

template-curly-spacing.description: Require or disallow spacing around embedded expressions of template strings in `<template>`

v-if-else-key.description: require key attribute for conditionally rendered repeated components

v-on-event-hyphenation.description: enforce v-on event naming style on custom components in template

v-on-handler-style.description: enforce writing style for handlers in `v-on` directives

valid-model-definition.description: require valid keys in model option
